Ayah

الٓمٓر ۚ تِلْكَ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ۗ وَٱلَّذِىٓ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ ٱلْحَقُّ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱)
Alif, Lam, Mim, Ra. These are the signs of the Book. That which has been sent down to you from your Lord is the truth, but most people do not believe [in it]. (1)

Messages

1- The Quran is a book of immense grandeur. «تلك»

2- The revelation of heavenly books is for the nurturing (and training) of people. «اُنزل الیك من ربّك»

3- Falsehood has no way into the Quran. «اُنزل الیك... الحق»

4- The criterion is truthfulness, not majority. The indifference and disbelief of the majority of people is not proof that the path is false. «الحقّ ولكن اكثر النّاس لایؤمنون»

5- The majority of people are not inclined toward the truth. «اكثر النّاس لایؤمنون»

6- The leader must know that many people will not believe in him, so he faces this issue with greater preparedness. (The overall concept of the Ayah)

Explanations

| This Surah was revealed in Mecca and contains forty-three Ayahs. It is named Ar-Ra'd (The Thunder) due to the mention of the subject of the glorification (Tasbih) of the thunder of the sky in Ayah 13 of this Surah. Topics concerning the grandeur of the Quran, Monotheism (Tawhid), the creation of the heavens and the earth, the subjugation of the moon and the sun, the creation of fruits and plants, the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and Divine Justice, the responsibility of the people, fulfilling covenants and maintaining kinship ties (Silat ar-Rahim), patience and abandoning vengeance, achieving true tranquility in the light of faith in Allah, and the ominous fate of the opponents of the Prophets علیهم السلام are included in this Surah.

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى رَفَعَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ بِغَيْرِ عَمَدٍ تَرَوْنَهَا ۖ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ۖ وَسَخَّرَ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ ۖ كُلٌّ يَجْرِى لِأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۚ يُدَبِّرُ ٱلْأَمْرَ يُفَصِّلُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لَعَلَّكُم بِلِقَآءِ رَبِّكُمْ تُوقِنُونَ (۲)
It is Allah who raised the heavens without any pillars that you see, and then presided over the Throne. He disposed the sun and the moon, each moving for a specified term. He directs the command, [and] elaborates the signs that you may be certain of encountering your Lord. (2)

Key Points

Verses in the Quran indicate that Allah maintains the heavens (and the earth), including: A: The verse above, «اللَّه الّذى رفع السّموات بغیر عمد ترونها» B: «ان اللَّه یمسك السّموات والارض ان تزولا ولئن زالتا ان امسكهما من احد من بعده»(1) Indeed, Allah protects the heavens and the earth from falling, and if they were to fall, no one but Him could hold them. C: «و یمسك السّماء ان تقع على الارض الاّ باذنه»(2) And Allah prevents the sky from falling upon the earth, except by His permission.

Allamah Tabataba'i (may Allah have mercy upon him) says in Tafsir al-Mizan: The meaning of tafsil (detailing/explaining) is the separation of the celestial spheres and the earth from one another, and observing this separation makes us aware of the power to separate people on the Day of Resurrection.

'Amad (عَمد) is the plural of 'amud, meaning pillar or support.

Imam Ali عليه السلام was asked how Allah settles the accounts of all creation. He replied: In the same way that He provides sustenance to all creation.(3)

1) Fatir, 41. 2) Hajj, 65. 3) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr al-Razi.

Messages

1- The heavens are established upon supports. «بغیر عمد ترونها» Not seeing something is not proof of its non-existence. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: «فثَمّ عَمَد ولكن لا ترونها»(4) There are supports, but you do not see them.

2- The sun is also in motion. «كلّ یجرى»

3- The celestial spheres are timed in the duration of their movement. «اجل مسمّىً»

4- Existence is under Divine management. «یدبّر»

5- Allah's hand is open in detailing the signs (whether detailing the legislative signs or detailing the cosmic signs). «یفصّل الآیات»

6- The cosmic order is purposeful. «یجرى لاجل - لعلكم بلقاء ربّكم توقنون» The creation of the world without the Resurrection is a vain and futile act.

7- The proof for the Resurrection is the same proof for Monotheism. The God Who creates, manages, and so on, will also establish the Day of Judgment. «رفع، استوى، یدبر،... بلقاء ربّكم توقنون»

4) Bihar, Vol. 60, p. 79.

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى مَدَّ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا رَوَٰسِىَ وَأَنْهَـٰرًا ۖ وَمِن كُلِّ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ جَعَلَ فِيهَا زَوْجَيْنِ ٱثْنَيْنِ ۖ يُغْشِى ٱلَّيْلَ ٱلنَّهَارَ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (۳)
It is He who has spread out the earth and set in it firm mountains and streams, and of every fruit in it He has made two kinds. He draws the night’s cover over the day. There are indeed signs in that for a people who reflect. (3)

Key Points

The previous Ayah was about the heavens, and this Ayah is about the earth and its earthly blessings. «رواسى» (Rawaasi) is the plural of «راسیه» (Raasiyah), meaning stability (ثبوت). For this reason, fixed and firm mountains are called Rawaasi.

«زوج» (Zauj) and «زوجان» (Zaujaan) both mean male and female. Although Linnaeus (لینه), the botanist, succeeded in discovering the law of pairing (زوجیّت) in plants in the 18th century, Islam referred to this point a thousand and several hundred years ago. Of course, people understood the issue of male and female in some plants, such as the date palm, but the universality of pairing (زوجیّت) was announced through the Quran. Male and female in plants are sometimes found in one tree or blossom, and sometimes in two trees or two blossoms. (5)

The phrase «مدّ الارض» (Madd al-Ard) perhaps refers to the spreading out of the earth from beneath the water, which is mentioned in narrations as "Daḥw al-Arḍ." This possibility corresponds with the theory of modern geologists who say that the earth was initially situated beneath the water. And Allah knows best.

The system of creation is based on pairing (زوجیّت): A: Pairing in vegetation; «وانبتت من كلّ زوج بهیج» (6) B: Pairing in animals; «و من الانعام ازواجاً» (7) C: Pairing in humans; «خلق لكم من انفسكم ازواجاً» (8) D: Pairing in everything; «و من كلّ شى ءٍ خلقنا زوجین» (9)

5) Tafsir Furqan. 6) Hajj, 5. 7) Shura, 11. 8) Rum, 21. 9) Dhariyat, 49.

Messages

1- The spreading out of the earth has been accomplished wisely and with careful planning. «مدّ الارض»

(The vastness of the earth and the existence of mountains upon it maintain the earth's balance and are a factor in human benefit, because if the entire earth were flat or entirely mountainous, life would not be possible.)

2- Mountains are a source of water storage, and rivers are a means of water distribution, and both pave the way for human life. «رواسى و انهاراً»

3- The succession and exchange of night and day are factors for life. «یغشى الّیل النّهار» (Otherwise, everything would either burn up due to heat, or wither and freeze due to the lack of light and warmth.)

4- The natural world is a classroom, not a house of heedlessness. «لقوم یتفكّرون»

Ayah

وَفِى ٱلْأَرْضِ قِطَعٌ مُّتَجَـٰوِرَٰتٌ وَجَنَّـٰتٌ مِّنْ أَعْنَـٰبٍ وَزَرْعٌ وَنَخِيلٌ صِنْوَانٌ وَغَيْرُ صِنْوَانٍ يُسْقَىٰ بِمَآءٍ وَٰحِدٍ وَنُفَضِّلُ بَعْضَهَا عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ فِى ٱلْأُكُلِ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ (٤)
In the earth are neighbouring terrains [of diverse kinds] and vineyards, farms, and date palms growing from the same root and from diverse roots, [all] irrigated by the same water, and We give some of them an advantage over others in flavour. There are indeed signs in that for people who exercise their reason. (4)

Key Points

The word "صنوان" (ṣinwān), contrary to its appearance which has the dual form, is the plural of "صِنو" (ṣinw), meaning a branch that emerges from the root of the tree. Here, it means similar or alike.

Messages

1- The earth consists of adjacent pieces, where each piece possesses its own specific potential. «قطع مجاورات»

2- The variety of fruits with different colors, tastes, smells, and shapes are all signs of Divine power. «لایاتٍ»

3- The variety of fruits is due to the will and desire of Allah; otherwise, a single type of water would only produce one type of taste. «یُسقى بماءٍ واحد»

4- Adjacency is not proof of uniformity. The criterion for superiority is greater productivity. «متجاورات - نفضّل فى الاُكل»

5- Thinkers strengthen and satisfy the spirit of their faith through edibles, while others merely suffice with filling their stomachs. «لایات لقوم یعقلون»

Ayah

۞ وَإِن تَعْجَبْ فَعَجَبٌ قَوْلُهُمْ أَءِذَا كُنَّا تُرَٰبًا أَءِنَّا لَفِى خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ ۗ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِرَبِّهِمْ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلْأَغْلَـٰلُ فِىٓ أَعْنَاقِهِمْ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (٥)
If you are to wonder [at anything], then wonderful is their remark, ‘When we have become dust, shall we be [ushered] into a new creation?’ They are the ones who defy their Lord; they shall have iron collars around their necks, they shall be the inhabitants of the Fire, and they shall remain in it [forever]. (5)

Key Points

This Ayah addresses the Prophet, saying: Do not be surprised by people's denial of prophethood, because they are also astonished by My power to resurrect the dead and do not believe in it. The deniers of the Resurrection have not presented any proof for its impossibility; they merely consider its occurrence unlikely. In contrast, the Quran, besides mentioning Allah's justice and wisdom which necessitate the existence of the Day of Judgment, has repeatedly responded to this skepticism of the deniers. In one place, He says: If you are in doubt about the occurrence of the Resurrection, recall your initial creation, how We created you from dust and then from a drop of semen. «یاایها النّاس ان كنتم فى ریب من البعث فانّا خلقناكم من تراب ثم من نطفة»(10) And in another place, He says: O Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله! Tell the people: The same One who created you the first time will create you again on the Day of Judgment, and there is no cause for astonishment. «قل یحییها الّذى أنشاها اوّل مرّة و هو بكلّ خلق علیم»(11)

10) Hajj, 5. 11) Yasin, 79.

Messages

1- Denying the Resurrection means denying the power, justice, and wisdom of Allah, and this is disbelief (kufr). «اولئك الّذین كفروا»

2- Since the denier of the Resurrection focuses entirely on this world, he becomes afflicted by the shackles of materialism, self-indulgence, ignorance, and superstitions. Such a person will also be in the shackles of divine punishment in the Hereafter. «الاغلال»

3- The shackles and chains on the Day of Judgment will not merely rest upon the necks of the deniers so that they can remove them; rather, they will be so deeply embedded in their necks that they cannot be taken off. «فى اعناقهم»

4- Since the denier of the Resurrection has not prepared provisions to save himself from divine wrath, he will remain in torment forever. «خالدین»

Ayah

وَيَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِٱلسَّيِّئَةِ قَبْلَ ٱلْحَسَنَةِ وَقَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِمُ ٱلْمَثُلَـٰتُ ۗ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو مَغْفِرَةٍ لِّلنَّاسِ عَلَىٰ ظُلْمِهِمْ ۖ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَشَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (٦)
They would press you for evil sooner than for good, though there have already gone by before them exemplary punishments. Indeed your Lord is forgiving to mankind despite their wrongdoing, and indeed your Lord is severe in retribution. (6)

Key Points

«مَثُلات» is the plural of «مثله», meaning the punishment and retribution that befalls a person. Sometimes obstinacy and stubbornness reach a point where a person is willing to wish for destruction but refuses to accept the truth. The Holy Quran refers to examples of such attitudes, including: The disbelievers used to say: «و اذ قالوا الّلهم اِن كان هذا هوالحقّ من عندك فامطر علینا حجارة من السّماء او ائتنا بعذاب الیم»(12) O Allah! If this Quran is the truth and from You, then either rain down stones upon us from the sky or afflict us with a painful punishment (for we will not accept it). In another place, it is stated: If We had revealed this Quran to a non-Arab (Ajam) and the Prophet recited it to the people: «ولو نزّلناه على بعض الاعجمین فقرأه علیهم ما كانوا به مؤمنین»(13) they would not have believed in it and would not have accepted it. Or that the People of the Book would say to the disbelievers and idolaters: «الم تر الى الّذین اوتوا نصیباً من الكتاب یؤمنون بالجبت و الطاغوت و یقولون للذین كفروا هؤلاء اهدى من الّذین آمنوا سبیلاً»(14) Your path is better than the path of Islam. Even though the People of the Book are closer to Islam than the polytheists, their stubbornness kept them from expressing the truth. The haste of some people for the descent of divine wrath may be due to these reasons: A: Negligence and forgetting the history of past generations, and disbelieving in or considering divine wrath unlikely. B: Envy towards the possessions of others. As mentioned in history, when the Commander of the Faithful, Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, attained the Imamate, a person who could not tolerate this wished for death, which is referred to in the occasion of the revelation (Sha’n Nuzul) of Surah Al-Ma'arij. C: Feeling disconnected, despair, and reaching a complete dead end. D: Mockery and refusal to accept, even at the cost of one's life. The Quran states: If Allah were to punish people immediately for their sins and bad deeds, no one would remain on earth, but He, out of kindness, waits so that people might repent. «ولو یؤاخذ اللَّه النّاس بظلمهم ما ترك علیها من دابّة و لكن یؤخّرهم الى اجل مسمّى...»(15) and the Ayah «ولو یؤاخذ اللَّه النّاس بما كسبوا ماترك على ظهرها من دابّة...»(16)

12) Anfal, 32. 13) Shu'ara, 198 - 199. 14) Nisa, 51. 15) Nahl, 61. 16) Fatir, 45.

Messages

1- Due to stubbornness, man falls so low that he is willing to be destroyed but refuses to accept the truth. «و یستعجلونك بالعذاب»

2- The history of previous generations is the best model and example. «قدخلت من قبلهم المثلات»

3- Do not consider divine wrath a joke, and pay attention to past examples. «من قبلهم المثلات»

4- Allah’s tradition (Sunnah) is to grant respite and is not concerned with people's haste. «لذو مغفرة»

5- The path of repentance and return is open even for stubborn individuals. «لذو مغفرة... على ظلمهم»

6- Allah still loves people despite all the evil that emanates from them. «لذو مغفرة للنّاس على ظلمهم»

7- Allah’s grace precedes His wrath. «مغفرة - عقاب»

8- Fear and hope (Khawf wa Raja), dread and expectation, together are factors for growth. «لذو مغفرة - لشدید العقاب»

9- Divine wrath and grace stem from His Lordship (Rububiyyah). «ربّك لذو مغفرة، ربّك لشدید العقاب»

Ayah

وَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مُنذِرٌ ۖ وَلِكُلِّ قَوْمٍ هَادٍ (۷)
The faithless say, ‘Why has not some sign been sent down to him from his Lord?’ You are only a warner, and there is a guide for every people. (7)

Key Points

It is narrated from Ibn Abbas that the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله placed His blessed hand on His chest and said: "I am the Warner (al-Mundhir)," and then pointed to Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام and said: "You are the Guide (al-Hadi); through you, the guided ones will be guided after me."(17)

17) Tafsir Kabir, Vol. 19, p. 14. Tabari, Ibn Kathir, and Ihqaq al-Haqq (Vol. 3, p. 87) have also narrated it.

Messages

1- The disbelievers have undue expectations from Allah and His Prophet. (They demand signs and miracles based on their own whims and desires.) «لولا انزل علیه ایة»

2- The fault-finding person is not worthy of divine address. «انّما انت» Allah left the speech of the disbelievers unanswered and addressed the Prophet instead.

3- Education requires both warning (threat) and guidance (counsel). «انمّا انت منذر و لكلّ قوم هاد»

4- For an ignorant and heedless society, warning is more necessary than glad tidings. «انما انت منذر»(18)

5- The main task of the prophets is guidance and warning; «انما انت منذر» not fulfilling the irrelevant demands of the people or presenting a miracle at every moment for everyone. «لولا انزل علیه آیة»

6- Although no other prophet will be sent, Allah completes His proof upon all people, and the earth is never left without a proof (Hujjah). «و لكلّ قومٍ هاد»

7- Prophets were not exclusive to the Eastern lands alone. «و لكلّ قوم هادٍ»

8- In the present age, too, we must have a living Imam or Guide (Hadi). «ولكلّ قوم هاد»(19) 18) The word "Inzar" (warning) has appeared about 120 times in the Quran, 100 of which were revealed in Mecca.

19) In the commentaries Majma' al-Bayan and Kanz al-Daqa'iq, 26 narrations state that the intent of "Hadi" (Guide) is the infallible Imams علیهم السلام.

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا تَحْمِلُ كُلُّ أُنثَىٰ وَمَا تَغِيضُ ٱلْأَرْحَامُ وَمَا تَزْدَادُ ۖ وَكُلُّ شَىْءٍ عِندَهُۥ بِمِقْدَارٍ (۸)
Allah knows what every female carries [in her womb], and what the wombs reduce and what they increase, and everything is by [precise] measure with Him, (8)

Key Points

In this Ayah, first, the knowledge of Allah regarding the bearing (carrying) of every female (whether possessing a womb, like humans and animals, or not possessing a womb, like plants and inanimate objects) is discussed, and then reference is made to beings that possess a womb. "غیض" (Ghayd) means the absorption (of the sperm/nutfah). This means that Allah is aware of the fluid that the womb absorbs, the changes it undergoes, and how it moves toward growth and increase.

Messages

1- Allah knows the reproductive states of all beings. «یعلم ما تحمل كل انثى»

2- Allah's knowledge extends even to the details. (He is aware of the lineage, characteristics, talents, shape, and gender of the fetus.) «یعلم... ماتغیض... وما تزداد»

3- The womb plays an important role in the perfection or deficiency of the newborn. «ما تغیض الارحام وماتزداد»

4- The system of creation for beings is based on precise measure and quantity. «بمقدار»

(Meaning, whether the duration of pregnancy is short or long, whether the pregnancy is aborted or results in multiple births, whether the fetus is malformed or has an extra limb, this decrease and increase [«تغیض و تزداد»] is based on a calculated formula.)

Ayah

عَـٰلِمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ ٱلْكَبِيرُ ٱلْمُتَعَالِ (۹)
the Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, the All-great, the All-sublime. (9)

Key Points

The manifest (the apparent) and the hidden (the unseen) are relevant concepts for the limited human being, whose five senses are even more restricted than those of many animals. However, for Allah, the Creator of the unseen (Ghayb) and the manifest (Shahadah), such a distinction has no meaning. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding "عالم الغیب و الشّهادة" (Knower of the Unseen and the Manifest): "الغیب ما لم یكن والشّهادة ما قد كان" (The Ghayb is that which has not yet been, and the Shahadah is that which has already been). Ghayb means something that has not existed, and Shahadah means something that has existed.(20)

(20) Tafsir Burhan.

Messages

1- Allah is Great ("Kabir") regarding every perfection, and He is Pure and Exalted ("Muta'al") regarding every flaw and defect.

Ayah

سَوَآءٌ مِّنكُم مَّنْ أَسَرَّ ٱلْقَوْلَ وَمَن جَهَرَ بِهِۦ وَمَنْ هُوَ مُسْتَخْفٍۭ بِٱلَّيْلِ وَسَارِبٌۢ بِٱلنَّهَارِ (۱٠)
It is the same [to Him] whether any of you speaks secretly, or does so loudly, or whether he lurks in the night, or is open to view in daytime. (10)

Key Points

This Ayah, which apparently explains and completes the previous Ayah, is one of the many verses in the Glorious Qur'an concerning Allah's knowledge of the small and large, apparent and hidden deeds, and even the thoughts and intentions of human beings. If humans believe in such knowledge from the Lord, it can be the best factor for modesty (Haya) and piety (Taqwa) and the greatest means of their education and refinement. This is because it leads to the encouragement of the righteous and the threat against the wicked. It is interesting that Allah, in this Ayah, mentions His knowledge of secret words and hidden, nocturnal deeds before mentioning public speech and manifest, daily actions. The word "sārib" (سارب), derived from "sarb" (سَرب), originally means flowing water, but it is also applied to someone who goes about a task during the day.

Messages

1- Allah's knowledge is uniform regarding everything. (It is not like our knowledge and awareness, which is greater concerning some things, less concerning others, or non-existent regarding some.) "سواء منكم ..."

Ayah

لَهُۥ مُعَقِّبَـٰتٌ مِّنۢ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِۦ يَحْفَظُونَهُۥ مِنْ أَمْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُغَيِّرُ مَا بِقَوْمٍ حَتَّىٰ يُغَيِّرُوا۟ مَا بِأَنفُسِهِمْ ۗ وَإِذَآ أَرَادَ ٱللَّهُ بِقَوْمٍ سُوٓءًا فَلَا مَرَدَّ لَهُۥ ۚ وَمَا لَهُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ مِن وَالٍ (۱۱)
He has guardian angels, to his front and his rear, who guard him by Allah’s command. Indeed Allah does not change a people’s lot, unless they change what is in their souls. And when Allah wishes to visit ill on a people, there is nothing that can avert it, and they have no protector besides Him. (11)

Key Points

Mu'aqqibāt (معقبات) is the plural of Mu'aqqibah (معّقبة). The letter tā' (تاء) in it is not for feminization but for emphasis (mubālaghah), like ‘allāmah (علامة). Therefore, the subject (fā'il) of yaḥfaẓūnahu (یحفظونه) is masculine. However, the intent of Mu'aqqibāt is not the pursuit of man, which would contradict bayna yadayhi (بین یدیه), but rather the succession and continuous coming of the angels day and night.

The intent of amr Allāh (امر اللَّه) in this Ayah is not divine wrath or punishment, because it makes no sense for angels to protect man against divine retribution. Rather, the intent is natural dangers and accidents, since nature is Allah's creation, and whatever happens within it is also by His will and decree.

In numerous verses and narrations, mention is made of angels who are assigned to protect man's life and record his deeds, and they guard him against dangers that are not subject to Allah's definite will. Of course, based on what is understood from the narrations, as soon as Allah's definitive will arrives, the guardian angels abandon their protective mission and surrender man to the inevitable divine term (ajal).

Therefore, Allah's command and decree are of two types: definite and non-definite (qaḍā' and qadar). The angels only protect man from non-definite occurrences. It is obvious that this protection does not negate man's free will, and the destiny of individuals and nations remains in their own hands.

The angels' protection covers both the lives of human beings and their deeds and conduct: «ان علیكم لحافظین - یرسل علیكم حفظة» (Indeed, over you are guardians - He sends over you guardians), and also their faith and thoughts against deviations and satanic whispers, because yaḥfaẓūnahu (یحفظونه) includes both the soul and the body.

Imam Sajjad عليه السلام, referring to this Ayah, said: The sins that change blessings are: oppressing people, being ungrateful to Allah, and abandoning good deeds that a person has become accustomed to. (21)

This Ayah pertains to human societies, not individual human beings. That is, a righteous society is covered by Allah's blessings, and a deviant society is afflicted by divine wrath. However, this rule does not necessarily apply to the righteous individual and the unrighteous person, because sometimes a righteous person may face difficulties due to divine testing, or an unrighteous person may be left alone due to divine respite.

21) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Allah does not abandon man. «له معقبات»

2- A group of divine angels are the protectors of human beings. «له معقبات»

3- Allah protects man from unexpected occurrences, «له معقبات یحفظونه», but not from occurrences they knowingly and intentionally bring upon themselves. «انّ اللَّه لایغیّر مابقوم»

4- Various dangers and occurrences surround man. «من بین یدیه ومن خلفه»

5- Allah does not withdraw a blessing He has bestowed unless people show ingratitude for that blessing. «انّ اللَّه لایغیّر ما بقوم»

6- Do not resort to luck, fortune, divination, or astrology; your destiny is in your own hands. «حتّى یغیّروا ما بانفسهم»

7- Allah's protection lasts as long as man does not commit ingratitude; otherwise, he will be deprived of divine grace and left to his own devices. «اذا اراد اللَّه بقوم سوءاً»

8- Enjoyment of outward and external blessings depends on spiritual perfections and inner states. «حتّى یغیّروا ما بانفسهم» In another place, He says: «و لو انّ اهل القرى آمنوا و اتَّقَوا لفتحنا علیهم بركات من السّماء و الارض...» (22)

9- Allah's will is above all other wills. «اذا اراد اللَّه بقوم سوءاً فلامردّ له» 22) Al-A'raf, 96.

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُرِيكُمُ ٱلْبَرْقَ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا وَيُنشِئُ ٱلسَّحَابَ ٱلثِّقَالَ (۱۲)
It is He who shows you the lightning, inspiring fear and hope, and He produces the clouds heavy [with rain]. (12)

Key Points

The lightning and the celestial bolt of the sky are a source of fear and terror for people from one perspective, due to untimely rainfall, the flow of floods, destruction, and fires. And from another perspective, they are a cause of joy, vitality, and hope, because they lead to the pouring down of rain, the watering of trees and crops, the freshness and vibrancy of the air, and cleanliness and purity.

Messages

  1. Although seemingly, natural factors are the basis for the appearance of thunder, lightning, and rain, the Originator and Creator of all factors is Allah. «ینشى ء السحاب الثقال»

  2. The discovery of the physical and chemical laws of nature should not diminish our faith in Allah, because nature and its laws are His creation. «یریكم البرق... ینشى ء السحاب»

Ayah

وَيُسَبِّحُ ٱلرَّعْدُ بِحَمْدِهِۦ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ مِنْ خِيفَتِهِۦ وَيُرْسِلُ ٱلصَّوَٰعِقَ فَيُصِيبُ بِهَا مَن يَشَآءُ وَهُمْ يُجَـٰدِلُونَ فِى ٱللَّهِ وَهُوَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْمِحَالِ (۱۳)
The Thunder celebrates His praise, and the angels [too], in awe of Him, and He releases the thunderbolts and strikes with them whomever He wishes. Yet they dispute concerning Allah, though He is great in might. (13)

Key Points

In the culture of the Quran, the entire existence is engaged in the glorification (Tasbih) of Allah, and this glorification is based on knowledge, consciousness, and choice. Interestingly, the Quran presents and expresses this matter in a way that draws minds to it and serves as a refutation of disbeliefs. Including: 1. The use of words like "سَبَّحَ" (sabbaha) and "يُسَبِّحُ" (yusabbihu), which explicitly convey this meaning. 2. The repetition of this matter in multiple Surahs. 3. Presenting the issue of the glorification of creatures at the beginning of the Surah, immediately after "بِسْمِ اللَّهِ" (Bismillah). 4. The use of words such as: the devotion and humility (Qunut and Khudu') of all existence, "كُلٌّ لَهُ قَانِتُونَ" (23); the prostration (Sujud) of stars and plants, "وَالنَّجْمُ وَالشَّجَرُ يَسْجُدَانِ" (24); the obedience of the heavens and the earth, "قَالَتَا أَتَيْنَا طَائِعِينَ" (25); the awareness of creatures in prayer and glorification, "كُلٌّ قَدْ عَلِمَ صَلَاتَهُ وَتَسْبِيحَهُ" (26). 5. Addressing humanity that you do not comprehend their glorification, "لَا تَفْقَهُونَ تَسْبِيحَهُمْ" (27).

"مِحَال" (Mihal) is derived from "حِيلَة" (hilah), meaning any kind of hidden strategizing or planning. And since strategizing necessitates knowledge and power, commentators have therefore interpreted "شَدِيدُ الْمِحَالِ" (Shadid ul-Mihal) as "intense in power and punishment" (شديد القوة و العذاب).

In some verses of the Quran, the glorification (Tasbih) and praise (Hamd) of Allah are placed side by side: "يُسَبِّحُ الرَّعْدُ بِحَمْدِهِ" (The thunder glorifies [Him] with His praise), "وَإِنْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا يُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِهِ" (28) (And there is not a thing but glorifies [Him] with His praise). Just as we say in the remembrance (Dhikr) of Ruku' (bowing) and Sujud (prostration): "سُبْحَانَ رَبِّيَ الْعَظِيمِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ" (Glory be to my Lord, the Great, and praise be to Him) and "سُبْحَانَ رَبِّيَ الْأَعْلَى وَبِحَمْدِهِ" (Glory be to my Lord, the Most High, and praise be to Him).

It is mentioned in several narrations from the Ahl al-Sunnah that the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله would interrupt His speech upon hearing the sound of thunder and engage in supplication, and He would also guide others to do this (29).

Some thunderbolts (Sawa'iq) are divine retribution and punishment that descend upon sinful nations, such as the people of Thamud: "...فَأَخَذَتْهُم صَاعِقَةُ الْعَذَابِ الْهُونِ بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ" (30) (...so the thunderbolt of humiliating punishment seized them because of what they used to earn).

23) Al-Baqarah, 116. 24) Ar-Rahman, 6. 25) Fussilat, 11. 26) An-Nur, 41. 27) Al-Isra, 44. 28) Al-Isra, 44. 29) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur. 30) Fussilat, 17.

Messages

1- Thunder, like the angel, has a conscious glorification (Tasbih) based on awareness, because the words 'thunder' and 'angel' are mentioned side by side. "يُسَبِّحُ الرَّعْدُ بِحَمْدِهِ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ"

2- The glorification of the angels is based on the fear and awe of Allah (Khishyah). "مِنْ خِيفَتِهِ"

3- Thunderbolts and being struck by them are not random occurrences; rather, they are by the will of Allah and according to divine laws. "يُرْسِلُ الصَّوَاعِقَ فَيُصِيبُ بِهَا مَنْ يَشَاءُ"

4- For the obstinate person, even a dangerous thing like a thunderbolt is not a deterrent. "يُجَادِلُونَ"

5- Do not contemplate trickery, deceit, or dispute with Allah, for one cannot contend with Him. "هُوَ شَدِيدُ الْمِحَالِ"

6- Not every kind of planning (Tadbir) or strategy (Hilah) is bad; perhaps the most severe planning and strategizing must be employed against certain individuals. "شَدِيدُ الْمِحَالِ"

Ayah

لَهُۥ دَعْوَةُ ٱلْحَقِّ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦ لَا يَسْتَجِيبُونَ لَهُم بِشَىْءٍ إِلَّا كَبَـٰسِطِ كَفَّيْهِ إِلَى ٱلْمَآءِ لِيَبْلُغَ فَاهُ وَمَا هُوَ بِبَـٰلِغِهِۦ ۚ وَمَا دُعَآءُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ إِلَّا فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ (۱٤)
[Only] to Him belongs the true invocation; and those whom they invoke besides Him do not answer them in any wise—like someone who stretches his hands towards water [desiring] that it should reach his mouth, but it does not reach it—and the invocations of the faithless only go awry. (14)

Key Points

The call to worship Allah, to turn away from focusing on others, and to consider Him alone as the effective power, has been repeatedly emphasized in the Quran. For instance: If man asks Me alone, I will surely answer him. «اجیب دعوة الداّع اذا دعان»(31) But if he goes to others and asks them for a need, he should know that they do not hear, and even if they did hear his supplication, they would not respond to him. «ان تدعوهم لایسمعوا و لو سمعوا دعائكم ما استجابوا لكم»(32) Limited man, living in the eventful life of this world, needs a secure refuge. The prophets introduce the true refuge to him, «له دعوة الحق». However, the help provided by others (the Taghut—tyrants/false deities) is either for stupefaction, exploitation, propaganda, maintaining their own position, or... and in reality, what is not important to them is man himself. Man is inherently thirsty for truth and seeks reality «لیبلغ فاه», but he loses the path to reach it. But nothing else—except faith in Allah, love for Him, intimacy with Him, and supplication and request from Him—can satisfy the infinitely seeking human being. «و ما هو ببالغه» This is because anything other than Allah is a mirage, and supplication to other than Him is futile.

31) Baqarah, 186. 32) Fatir, 14.

Messages

1- Do not turn to others besides Allah, for nothing can be accomplished by anyone other than Him. «لایستجیبون لهم بشى ءٍ»

2- The source of people's polytheism (shirk) is their false perceptions and imaginations. «كباسط كفیّه الى الماء... و ما هو ببالغه»

3- Whoever sincerely calls upon Allah returns with full hands. Returning empty-handed is due to turning attention toward others besides Allah. «له دعوة الحق... و ما دعاء الكافرین الاّ فى ضلال»

Ayah

وَلِلَّهِ يَسْجُدُ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ طَوْعًا وَكَرْهًا وَظِلَـٰلُهُم بِٱلْغُدُوِّ وَٱلْـَٔاصَالِ ۩ (۱٥)
To Allah prostrates whoever there is in the heavens and the earth, willingly or unwillingly, and their shadows at sunrise and sunset. (15)

Key Points

Man (مَنْ) is used for intelligent beings, and Ma (ما) is used for non-intelligent beings. However, in other verses similar to this one, Ma (ما) is used, such as the verse: «وللّه یسجد ما فى السّموات و ما فى الارض» (33). Therefore, it can be concluded that the intent of this verse is also the prostration (sajdah) of all beings, especially since dhilāl (ظلال - shadows) is mentioned, and it is obvious that a shadow does not possess intellect. Perhaps the meaning of the prostration of shadows is the state of the shadow falling upon the ground, similar to the analogy we use in Persian: "The cypress tree rides mounted, the bud arrives on foot." Āsāl (آصال) is the plural of usul (اُصُل), which is the plural of asīl (اصیل), derived from the root asl (اصل), meaning the end or the last part of the day.

33) An-Nahl, 49.

Messages

  1. The universe is obedient and prostrates to Allah; why should we not be? «و للَّه یسجد من...»

  2. Prostration (Sajdah) belongs exclusively to Allah. «وللَّه یسجد»

  3. Believers prostrate willingly and eagerly, but others are compelled to show humility and submission due to necessity. «طوعاً و كرهاً»

  4. If a being becomes devoted to Allah, its effects and consequences also align with the path of Allah. «ظلالهم»

  5. The prostration of beings is continuous. «یسجد... بالغدو و الاصال»

Ayah

قُلْ مَن رَّبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ ۚ قُلْ أَفَٱتَّخَذْتُم مِّن دُونِهِۦٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ لِأَنفُسِهِمْ نَفْعًا وَلَا ضَرًّا ۚ قُلْ هَلْ يَسْتَوِى ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ وَٱلْبَصِيرُ أَمْ هَلْ تَسْتَوِى ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتُ وَٱلنُّورُ ۗ أَمْ جَعَلُوا۟ لِلَّهِ شُرَكَآءَ خَلَقُوا۟ كَخَلْقِهِۦ فَتَشَـٰبَهَ ٱلْخَلْقُ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ خَـٰلِقُ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَهُوَ ٱلْوَٰحِدُ ٱلْقَهَّـٰرُ (۱٦)
Say, ‘Who is the Lord of the heavens and the earth?’ Say, ‘Allah!’ Say, ‘Have you then taken others besides Him for guardians, who have no control over their own benefit or harm?’ Say, ‘Are the blind one and the seer equal? Are darkness and light equal?’ Have they set up for Allah partners who have created like His creation, so that the creations seemed confusable to them? Say, ‘Allah is the creator of all things, and He is the One and the All-paramount.’ (16)

Key Points

Presenting issues in the form of question and answer is one of the methods of propagation, education, and instruction that the Quran has paid great attention to: "Say, who..." It is narrated in traditions (Riwāyāt) that polytheism (Shirk) towards Allah is more hidden than the movement of a black ant on a black stone on a dark night. (34) An example of this is when you say: "This deed was accomplished through the blessing of Allah and so-and-so."

34) Bihār, Vol. 72, p. 93.

Messages

1- Due to extreme fanaticism, the polytheists do not answer even the clearest questions; therefore, the Prophet must provide the answer to His own question. «من ربّ السّموات... قل اللَّه»

2- The disbelievers only accepted the Creatorship (Khāliqiyyah) of Allah, «لئن سئلتهم من خلق... لیقولن اللَّه» (35), but they considered others to be the planners/administrators (Mudabbir). «قل من ربّ...»

3- A human being who cannot attract benefit or repel harm for himself should not, under any title, be obeyed by people instead of Allah. «لایملكون لانفسهم نفعاً و لا ضرّاً»

4- The person who saw the truth but did not accept it has a blind inner eye, and the space around him is darkness (Ẓulumāt). «هل یستوى الاعمى والبصیر ...»

5- No one besides Allah has created anything, such that we might become confused regarding the creations. «فتشابه الخلق علیهم» 35) Al-Ankabūt, 61.

Ayah

أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَسَالَتْ أَوْدِيَةٌۢ بِقَدَرِهَا فَٱحْتَمَلَ ٱلسَّيْلُ زَبَدًا رَّابِيًا ۚ وَمِمَّا يُوقِدُونَ عَلَيْهِ فِى ٱلنَّارِ ٱبْتِغَآءَ حِلْيَةٍ أَوْ مَتَـٰعٍ زَبَدٌ مِّثْلُهُۥ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَضْرِبُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْحَقَّ وَٱلْبَـٰطِلَ ۚ فَأَمَّا ٱلزَّبَدُ فَيَذْهَبُ جُفَآءً ۖ وَأَمَّا مَا يَنفَعُ ٱلنَّاسَ فَيَمْكُثُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَضْرِبُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْأَمْثَالَ (۱۷)
He sends down water from the sky whereat the valleys are flooded to [the extent of] their capacity, and the flood carries along a swelling scum. And from what they smelt in the fire for the purpose of [making] ornaments or wares, [there arises] a similar scum. That is how Allah compares truth and falsehood. As for the scum, it leaves as dross, and that which profits the people stays in the earth. That is how Allah draws comparisons. (17)

Key Points

In this Ayah, two examples are mentioned to illustrate falsehood (Bāṭil): first, the example of the foam that appears on water, and second, the foam that covers molten metals when they are melted. Falsehood is like foam because: 1. It is transient (or ephemeral). 2. It manifests in the shadow of the truth. 3. It covers the surface of the truth. 4. It has an appearance but no value. It neither quenches the thirst of the thirsty nor does any plant grow from it. 5. It vanishes when conditions calm down. 6. It is high-sitting and noisy, but hollow and content-less.

As this matter is emphasized in other verses as well: «بل نقذف بالحقّ على الباطل فیدمغه»(36), We strike the falsehood with the truth. «یمح اللَّه الباطل»(37), Allah obliterates falsehood. «و ما یبدى ء الباطل و مایعید»(38), Falsehood is obliterated and destroyed in this world and the Hereafter. «انّ الباطل كان زهوقا»(39), Indeed, falsehood is transient. «جاء الحقّ و زهق الباطل»(40), The truth has come, and falsehood has vanished.

Analogy (or Parable) makes intellectual matters tangible, shortens the path to the goal, makes concepts universal, and silences the obstinate. Therefore, the Quran has frequently utilized this method.

36) انبیاء، 18. 37) شورى، 24. 38) سبأ، 49. 39) اسراء، 81. 40) اسراء، 81.

Messages

  1. Divine grace is flowing, and everyone benefits according to their capacity. «اودیة بقدرها»

  2. Effort to manufacture necessary goods (matā‘) or luxury items (ḥilyah) is commendable. «یوقدون علیه فى النّار»

  3. It is in the furnace of events that impurities become known and the truth and falsehood become manifest. «یوقدون علیه... زبدٌ مثله»

  4. The existence of falsehood is not restricted to a specific place or context. (Foam appears both on cold floodwater and on molten metals.) «زبد مثله»

  5. The source of falsehood is the impurities that have contaminated the truth and reality. «یضرب اللَّه الحقّ و الباطل»

  6. Truth and falsehood coexist, but ultimately, falsehood is transient. «فیذهب جُفاءً»

  7. The benefit of the people lies in the truth. «ما ینفع النّاس»

  8. The truth is lasting. «فیمكث فى الارض»

  9. Any wealth, knowledge, friend, or government that is beneficial to the people is lasting. «ما ینفع النّاس فیمكث فى الارض»

  10. What is sent down by Allah is pure and true, like rain, but as soon as it mixes with material things, it becomes subject to impurity and provides the ground for the appearance of foam and falsehood. «انزل من السّماء ماءً»

Ayah

لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَجَابُوا۟ لِرَبِّهِمُ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ لَمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ لَهُۥ لَوْ أَنَّ لَهُم مَّا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا وَمِثْلَهُۥ مَعَهُۥ لَٱفْتَدَوْا۟ بِهِۦٓ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ سُوٓءُ ٱلْحِسَابِ وَمَأْوَىٰهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمِهَادُ (۱۸)
For those who answer [the summons of] their Lord there shall be the best [of rewards]. But those who do not answer Him, even if they possessed all that is on the earth and as much of it besides, they would surely offer it to redeem themselves with it. For such there shall be an adverse reckoning, and their refuge shall be hell, and it is an evil resting place. (18)

Key Points

It is understood from the Quran that there are several types of reckoning (Hisab) for people on the Day of Judgment: 1. A group will have an easy reckoning. «حساباً یسیرا» (41) 2. A group will be strictly and precisely judged. «حساباً شدیداً» (42), «سوء الحساب». The meaning of «سوءالحساب» is strictness in the reckoning. 3. Some will go to Hell without reckoning and will not need trial or the Balance. «فلانقیم لهم یوم القیامة وزناً» (43) 4. A group will go to Paradise without reckoning. «انما یوفى الصّابرون اجرهم بغیر حساب» (44)

According to what is derived from numerous narrations and Hadiths, those who treat people with forgiveness and mercy will have an easy reckoning, and those who deal with people with severity and extreme strictness will have a difficult reckoning. Polytheists will go to Hell without reckoning, and the people of patience (the steadfast) will also go to Paradise without reckoning. (45)

In the Quran, response (Istijabah) is reciprocal. That is, if people expect a response from Allah, they must respond to the call of Allah. «استجیبوا للَّه و للرسول اذا دعاكم لما یحییكم» (46) Whenever Allah and the Messenger call you to the life-giving doctrine, respond positively so that Allah may also answer your prayer. «فاستجبناله» (47), «فاستجاب لهم ربّهم» (48)

A Discussion Concerning Invitation: The issue of invitation (Da'wah) can be discussed and considered from various dimensions:

  1. The Callers: Callers to the Truth: A: Prophets. «ادعو الى اللَّه» (49), «والرسول یدعوكم» (50), «داعیاً الى اللَّه باذنه» (51) B: Believers. «ولتكن منكم أمة یدعون الى الخیر» (52) C: Jinn. «یا قومنا اجیبوا داعى اللَّه» (53) But the Callers to Falsehood: A: Leaders of disbelief. «ائمة یدعون الى النّار» (54) B: Satan. «كان الشّیطان یدعوهم» (55), «ماكان لى علیكم من سلطان الاّ ان دعوتكم» (56) C: Polytheists. «اولئك یدعون الى النّار» (57)

  2. The Subject of the Call: A: Life. «دعاكم لما یحییكم» (58) B: The Straight Path. «لتدعوهم الى صراط مستقیم» (59) C: Forgiveness. «واللَّه یدعو الى الجنة والمغفرة» (60) D: Paradise. «واللَّه یدعوا الى دارالسلام» (61) E: Salvation. «ادعوكم الى النجاة» (62)

  3. The Reaction of Opponents: A: Accusation: Sorcerer; «ان هذا لساحرٌ علیم» (63) Poet; «بل هو شاعر» (64) Soothsayer; «فما انت بنعمة ربّك بكاهن» (65) Madman; «و یقولون انه لمجنون» (66) Liar; «لنظنّك من الكاذبین» (67) Power-seeker; «یرید ان یتفضّل علیكم» (68) B: Threat. «لرجمناك» (69), «او یقتلوك ...» (70) C: Humiliation. «أهذا الّذى ...» (71) D: Doubt. «أتعلمون ان صالحا مرسل من ربّه» (72) E: Plotting and War. «واذ یمكر بك الّذین كفروا لیثبتوك او یقتلوك او یخرجوك» (73)

  4. Motives and Factors for Non-Acceptance: A: Imitation (Blind following). B: Fanaticism. C: Arrogance. D: Vain desires. «فان لم یستجیبوا لك فاعلم انما یتبعون اهواءهم» (74)

  5. Reward for Acceptance: A: Recompense (Ajr). «و یزیدهم من فضله» (75) B: Life. «دعاكم لما یحییكم» (76) C: The Best (Al-Husna). «للذین استجابوا لربّهم الحسنى» (77)

Messages

  1. The best things await the believer. «الحسنى»

  2. Ransom or redemption will not exist on the Day of Judgment. «لافتدوا به»

  3. Acceptance of Allah's invitation must be based on willingness and desire. «استجابوا»

Ayah

۞ أَفَمَن يَعْلَمُ أَنَّمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ ٱلْحَقُّ كَمَنْ هُوَ أَعْمَىٰٓ ۚ إِنَّمَا يَتَذَكَّرُ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (۱۹)
Is someone who knows that what has been sent down to you from your Lord is the truth, like someone who is blind? Only those who possess intellect take admonition (19)

Key Points

The previous Ayah referred to those who respond to the life-giving call of the Prophets (علیهم السلام). This Ayah and the subsequent Ayahs explain the effects of responding to that call. Each of the innate nature (Fitrah), intellect, and knowledge in man possesses a core (maghz) and an essence (lubb), which are sometimes covered by habits, customs, superstitions, and instincts. Therefore, man must constantly pay attention to that central nucleus and their core essence. (78)

Characteristics of the Wise in the Quran

The term «اولوا الالباب» (those of understanding) appears [many] times in the Quran, and each time it is mentioned alongside a specific perfection or characteristic. Including:

  1. They understand the secrets of divine laws. «و لكم فى القصاص حیاة یا اولى الالباب» (79)
  2. They are farsighted (future-oriented). «تزودّوا فانّ خیر الزّاد التقوى واتقون یا اولى الالباب» (80)
  3. They consider the world a place of passage and transit, not a stopping point or destination. «لاولى الالباب الّذین... یتفكّرون فى خلق السّموات والارض ربّنا ما خلقت هذا باطلا» (81)
  4. They take lessons from history. «لقد كان فى قصصهم عبرة لاولى الالباب» (82)
  5. They accept the best and most superior logic. «الّذین یستمعون القول فیتبعون احسنه ... و اولئك هم اولوا الالباب» (83)
  6. They are devoted to night vigil (Tahajjud) and worship. «امّن هو قانت آناء الّیل... انما یتذكّر اولوا الالباب» (84)

Messages

  1. The Quran considers the person unaware of the truth to be blind. Instead of saying «كمن هو لایعلم» (like one who does not know), it says «كمن هو اعمى» (like one who is blind).
  2. Religion corresponds to the innate nature (Fitrah) of humans; only remembrance (Tadhakkur) is necessary to remove heedlessness. «انما یتذكّر...»
  3. Intellect that does not lead man to the truth of the Divine Book is not true intellect. «انّما یتذكّر اولوا الالباب»
  4. Beneficial knowledge is the product of reasoning (Ta'aqqul) and remembrance (Tadhakkur). «من یعلم، یتذكّر اولوا الالباب»
  5. To achieve sound intellect and pure innate nature (Fitrah), one must distance oneself from sin and heedlessness. «انما یتذكّر اولوا الالباب»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يُوفُونَ بِعَهْدِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا يَنقُضُونَ ٱلْمِيثَـٰقَ (۲٠)
—those who fulfill Allah’s covenant and do not break the pledge solemnly made, (20)

Key Points

The "Covenant of Allah" ('Ahd Allah) includes innate covenants, such as the love for truth and justice; it encompasses intellectual covenants, such as the understanding of the realities of the universe, the Origin (Mabda'), and the Return (Ma'ad); it applies to religious covenants, such as performing obligations (Wajibat) and abandoning prohibitions (Muharramat); and it is also true for covenants that people make and commit to with one another, the observance of which Allah has made obligatory.(85) One of the most important divine covenants is the Imamate (leadership) of the heavenly leaders. After Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام attained the rank of Imamate by passing numerous tests, he asked Allah that his descendants also possess this rank. To clarify the status of the Imamate, instead of saying that the rank of Imamate does not reach the unjust, Allah said: «لاینال عهدى الظالمین» (My covenant does not reach the wrongdoers/oppressors). Mithaq (solemn covenant) refers to something with which the human heart finds trust (wuthuq) and assurance. Since the presence of a divine leader calms and assures the hearts and souls of people, it is therefore considered one of the manifestations of Mithaq. Fulfilling a covenant, like respecting parents and returning trusts, is not merely an Islamic right, but a human right. Therefore, every wise and rational person must observe it.

Messages

1- A sound and healthy intellect makes a person loyal to the Divine religion. «اولوا الالباب، الذین یوفون»

2- Fulfilling the covenant is a result of wisdom and intellect. «اولواالالباب - یوفون ...»

3- Respecting social covenants and contracts is among the characteristics of a wise believing person. «لا ینقضون المیثاق»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ مَآ أَمَرَ ٱللَّهُ بِهِۦٓ أَن يُوصَلَ وَيَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ وَيَخَافُونَ سُوٓءَ ٱلْحِسَابِ (۲۱)
and those who join what Allah has commanded to be joined, and fear their Lord, and are afraid of an adverse reckoning (21)

Key Points

Narrations state that what Allah has commanded to be joined includes Silat al-Rahim (maintaining kinship ties), meaning preserving family bonds, and also preserving ideological bonds, meaning deep and continuous connection with the heavenly leaders and following the path of Wilayah (guardianship/leadership). (86) With a simple and fleeting glance at the world in the present era, we will see that despite having the best and most abundant capital—oil—beneath their feet, and having the best point of unity and love for over a billion Muslims—the Ka'bah—before them, and possessing the best ideology and logic, they still live under various pressures from the superpowers due to cutting off their connection with the issue of Divine leadership.

This is why in Ayah 27 of Surah Al-Baqarah, the phrase "و یفسدون فى الارض" (and they cause corruption on the earth) follows the phrase "و یقطعون ما امراللَّه به ان یوصل" (and they cut off what Allah has commanded to be joined). This is because it is obvious that cutting off kinship ties alone does not cause corruption on the earth; rather, it is abandoning Divine leadership that leads to falling into the clutches of the Taghut (tyrants) and the spread of corruption and decay.

The connections that Allah has commanded are numerous and diverse, including: A: Cultural connection with scholars. "فَسْئَلُوا أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ" (87) B: Social connection with people. "اصْبِرُوا وَ صَابِرُوا وَ رَابِطُوا" (88) C: Emotional connection with parents. "وَ بِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا" (89) D: Financial connection with the needy. "مَنْ ذَا الَّذِي يُقْرِضُ اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا" (90) E: Intellectual connection in managing society. "وَ شَاوِرْهُمْ فِي الْأَمْرِ" (91) F: Political connection with Wilayah. "وَ أَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَ أُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْكُمْ" (92) G: Comprehensive connection with believers. "إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِخْوَةٌ" (93) H: Spiritual connection with the friends of Allah. "لَقَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ فِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ" (94)

Silat al-Rahim Silat al-Rahim is not limited to visiting and meeting; financial aid is also considered an instance of Silat al-Rahim. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Besides Zakat, other rights are obligatory upon a person's wealth, and then He recited this Ayah. (95) Of course, perhaps the Imam عليه السلام meant Khums (the fifth) by "other rights."

Regarding the importance of Silat al-Rahim, it is sufficient that Allah has placed it alongside the mention of Himself. "وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ الَّذِي تَسَاءَلُونَ بِهِ وَالْأَرْحَامَ" (96)

Arham (kinship ties) is not limited only to family and blood relatives, but also encompasses the greater Islamic society in which all members of the Ummah are brothers, "إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِخْوَةٌ" (97), and whose fathers are the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله and Hadrat Ali عليه السلام. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "أَنَا وَ عَلِيٌّ أَبَوَا هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ" (Ali and I are the fathers of this Ummah). (98)

On the verge of His passing from this world, Imam Sadiq عليه السلام ordered that a gift be given to relatives who had insulted Him. When the Imam was criticized for doing this, He recited the above Ayah. (99) And in this way, He taught us that the condition for Silat al-Rahim is not their goodwill, affection, or relationship with us.

In explaining the phrases "يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ" (they fear their Lord) and "يَخَافُونَ سُوءَ الْحِسَابِ" (they fear the evil reckoning), it must be said that although the words khashyah (reverential fear) and khawf (fear) are sometimes used in a synonymous or similar meaning, and one meaning is intended from them, there is actually a difference between the two. This difference is that khashyah is the fear and emotional impact that a person feels in their heart due to respect and awe for someone, while khawf has a broader meaning and includes any kind of fear or anxiety. In other words, khashyah is never used regarding a bitter incident, and a person does not say, "I have khashyah of the cold, illness, etc.," whereas the use of the word khawf in matters such as fear of cold, heat, or illness is common and widespread. Finally, since khashyah is based on a person's knowledge of the greatness, importance, and respect of the other party, it can be said that khashyah is specific to knowledgeable individuals, while khawf is used for all people. As the Quran states: "إِنَّمَا يَخْشَى اللَّهَ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ الْعُلَمَاءُ" (100) Only the knowledgeable among His servants have khashyah of Allah.

Messages

1- Maintaining ideological and Divine bonds is a sign of sound intellect. "أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ... الَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ"

2- We must establish connections with relatives because of Allah's command, whether others seek connection with us or not. "أَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِأَنْ يُوصَلَ"

3- During visits and Silat al-Rahim, be mindful of sins, including lustful gazing. "يَصِلُونَ... يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ"

4- Fear Divine greatness ("يَخْشَوْنَ") and also fear His punishment ("يَخَافُونَ").

5- Cutting off kinship ties leads to a difficult reckoning. "يَصِلُونَ... يَخَافُونَ سُوءَ الْحِسَابِ"

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا۟ ٱبْتِغَآءَ وَجْهِ رَبِّهِمْ وَأَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَأَنفَقُوا۟ مِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً وَيَدْرَءُونَ بِٱلْحَسَنَةِ ٱلسَّيِّئَةَ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ عُقْبَى ٱلدَّارِ (۲۲)
—those who are patient for the sake of their Lord’s pleasure, maintain the prayer, and spend secretly and openly out of what We have provided them, and repel evil [conduct] with good. For such will be the reward of the [ultimate] abode: (22)

Key Points

"Patience" (sabr) does not only mean enduring hardships, but also includes steadfastness in worship, confronting sin, forbearance during calamity, submission in obedience, and avoiding arrogance and intoxication during times of blessing. "وجه ربّهم" (the Countenance of their Lord) means attracting the attention, favor, and satisfaction of the Lord. Establishing prayer (Iqamat al-Salat) is one of the manifestations of the divine covenants ("عهداللَّه") mentioned in previous verses, just as it is stated in a narration: "الصّلاة عهد اللَّه" (Prayer is the covenant of Allah). Patience (sabr) and prayer (salat) are connections with the Creator; spending (in charity) (infaq) and good deeds (hasanah) are connections with the people. Spending (in charity) has degrees: The first step: Giving from the bounty and provisions granted by Allah. "انفقوا مما رزقناكم" (101) The second step: Giving from one's earnings and lawful acquisition. "انفقوا من طیبات ما كسبتم" (102) The third step: Giving from what they love. "لن تنالوا البّر حتّى تنفقوا مما تحبّون" (103) The fourth step: Altruism (selflessness). "و یؤثرون على انفسهم و لو كان بهم خصاصه" (104) According to Al-Mizan, "عقبى الدار" (the ultimate outcome of the abode) means a good end in this world, but it can be said that it includes both realms: "this world and the Hereafter." The meaning of repelling evil with good is that if one of the believers commits an inappropriate act against us, we should overlook it, but this behavior should not be adopted toward oppressive and corrupt individuals, because in their case, retaliation (or reciprocal action) is necessary. In any case, although Islam is a religion of ethics, compassion, and forgiveness, it also includes, where appropriate, "لاتأخذكم بهما رأفة" (105). Islam is a comprehensive school of thought, and the "اولواالالباب" (people of understanding) are complete individuals. The presence of phrases such as: "یوفون" (they fulfill), "یصلون" (they connect), "یخشون" (they are reverent), "یخافون" (they fear), "صبروا" (they are patient), "اقاموا" (they establish), "انفقوا" (they spend), and "یدرؤن" (they repel) indicates that complete human beings are committed to their covenants, maintain all their connections, possess high piety, and, instead of isolation, are present and active in all fields.

Messages

1- Patience and steadfastness are valuable only when they are for Allah and in His path. (Not every fanaticism, obstinacy, or stubbornness.) "صبروا ابتغاء وجه ربّهم"

2- Prayer must be established, not merely performed (read). That is, its etiquette, conditions, limits, and instructions must be observed. "اقاموا الصّلاة"

3- Connection with God is not acceptable without helping others, although help is not limited to financial aid. "اقاموا الصّلاة و انفقوا"

4- Do not be arrogant about your spending; what you give is from Him. "انفقوا ممّا رزقناهم"

5- When the intention of the deed is "ابتغاء وجه ربّ" (seeking the Countenance of the Lord), meaning the satisfaction of the Lord, then whether the action is hidden or manifest is no longer important. "سراً و علانیةً"

6- Wisdom dictates that some aid should be public and others hidden. "سرّاً وعلانیةً"

7- We should compensate for sin with repentance, for evil with good, for oppression with forgiveness, for insult with peace (salutation), for punishment with charity, for harshness with connection (maintaining ties), for tyranny with consultation, and for the guardianship of the Taghut (tyrant) and Satan with the guardianship of the Truth. "یدرون بالحسنة السّیّئة"

8- A good outcome in this world and the Hereafter belongs to the people of understanding. "اولوا الالباب... اولئك لهم عقبى الدار"

Ayah

جَنَّـٰتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَهَا وَمَن صَلَحَ مِنْ ءَابَآئِهِمْ وَأَزْوَٰجِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّـٰتِهِمْ ۖ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ يَدْخُلُونَ عَلَيْهِم مِّن كُلِّ بَابٍ (۲۳)
the Gardens of Eden, which they will enter along with whoever is righteous from among their forebears, their spouses, and their descendants, and the angels will call on them from every door: (23)

Key Points

Out of the 127 times that Jannāt (Gardens) and Jannah (Garden) appear in the Quran, only 11 instances use the phrase Jannāt ‘Adn (Gardens of Eden), which, according to a Hadith from Durr al-Manthūr, means the special Paradise. (106) The Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever wishes for his life and death to be like my life and death, and to enter Jannāt ‘Adn, which is My Paradise, must take ‘Alī ibn Abī Ṭālib علیهما السلام and his progeny as his leader and Imam, for they are the best of mankind in knowledge and understanding, and the guides of people toward guidance. (107)

It has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran that the righteous members of a family will also be together in Paradise and will enjoy each other's company. Of course, the mention of specific family members like fathers, spouses, and children in the noble verse above does not imply exclusivity; it includes all righteous family members, encompassing those mentioned, as well as mothers, sisters, and brothers. Furthermore, perhaps the reason for not mentioning the mother's name is that the mother is the father's spouse and is included under azwāj (spouses), just as sisters and brothers are considered the progeny (dhurriyyah) of the father, and paternal uncles and aunts are counted among the descendants (dhurārī) of the fathers.

What is understood from the Divine verses is that angels maintain contact with humans in all circumstances, both in this world and the Hereafter (Barzakh and Qiyāmah/Resurrection). Sometimes they send blessings and salutations upon him, «يُصَلِّي عَلَيْكُمْ وَمَلَائِكَتُهُ» (108); sometimes they seek forgiveness for him, «وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا» (109); and sometimes they raise their hands in prayer for him, «رَبَّنَا وَأَدْخِلْهُمْ جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ» (110). At the time of death and the beginning of the world of Barzakh, they take their souls with the proclamation and instruction, «أَلَّا تَخَافُوا وَلَا تَحْزَنُوا» (111), and with the phrase, «سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ». «الَّذِينَ تَتَوَفَّاهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ طَيِّبِينَ يَقُولُونَ سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ» (112). And in the Hereafter, they greet them from every direction.

Eight gates have been mentioned for Paradise, which corresponds to the number of attributes enumerated for the Ulū al-Albāb (people of understanding) in the recent verses. It is as if each of these eight attributes opens a door of Paradise and a path toward eternal felicity for man.

Not every father and child, every husband and wife, or every ancestor and descendant will join each other and be together in Paradise, because on the Day of Resurrection, lineage (ansāb) and means (asbāb) are ineffective. «فَلَا أَنْسَابَ بَيْنَهُمْ» (113). On that Day, every soul is held hostage by its own deeds, «كُلُّ نَفْسٍ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ رَهِينَةٌ» (114), and nothing is effective except effort and striving, «لَيْسَ لِلْإِنْسَانِ إِلَّا مَا سَعَىٰ» (115). Therefore, the joining of some relatives with others is solely due to their individual merit and righteousness.

A similar verse can also be seen in Sūrah Ghāfir, verse 7, and Sūrah Ṭūr, verse 21.

Messages

1- The condition for entering Paradise is being righteous. «وَمَن صَلَحَ»

2- The heavenly family is one whose members maintain solidarity and intimacy upon the path of truth. «صَلَحَ مِنْ آبَائِهِمْ»

3- Attention to emotions and affections is a principle in Islam. For a person, living in the best places but away from family lacks serenity. «يَدْخُلُونَهَا وَمَن صَلَحَ مِنْ آبَائِهِمْ»

Ayah

سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْكُم بِمَا صَبَرْتُمْ ۚ فَنِعْمَ عُقْبَى ٱلدَّارِ (۲٤)
‘Peace be to you, for your patience.’ How excellent is the reward of the [ultimate] abode! (24)

Key Points

One of the unique distinctions of the Quran is its expression of the most profound content within the simplest and briefest words. Among these is the phrase "سلام علیكم" (Salāmun ‘alaykum), which is a very short and easy sentence, yet profound and eloquent, and its history reaches back to past Prophets like Abraham, Noah, and Adam علیهم السلام. «سلام على نوح فى العالمین»(116), «سلام على موسى وهارون»(117) Salām (peace) is one of the Names of Allah, the congratulation of Allah upon the Prophets, the divine blessing upon the inhabitants of Paradise, the whisper of the angels, the international word of all Muslims, the slogan of the people of Paradise in this world and the next, the remembrance of the Creator and the created, the call upon entry and exit, the beginning of every word in writing and speech, which is said to both the dead and the living, and to both the old and the young, and responding to it is obligatory in all circumstances. The message of Salām is respect, blessing, prayer, security, and congratulation from Allah. «سلام قولا من ربّ رحیم»(118) When we say at the end of every prayer: "السّلام علینا و على عباداللَّه الصّالحین" (Peace be upon us and upon the righteous servants of Allah), through this Salām we break down all racial boundaries and the hollow distinctions of age, gender, wealth, status, language, and time throughout history, and we establish connection with all the righteous servants of God and send greetings upon them.

It is narrated in traditions: A group of people will enter Paradise before their accounts are settled. When the angels ask the reason, they hear the reply that in the world, we were patient in obeying Allah and patient regarding difficulties and calamities; we were people of forbearance. Upon hearing the answer, the angels welcome them with "سلام علیكم..."(119)

Imam Sadiq علیه السلام said: We are the people of patience, but our followers (Shi'a) are more patient than us. This is because our patience is regarding something we know, but they are patient regarding something they do not know.(120)

Points regarding Patience

  1. We must know the origin and source of patience is from Allah. «و ما صبرك الا باللَّه»(121)
  2. We must also make the destination and goal of patience divine satisfaction, not good reputation or anything else. «و لربّك فاصبر»(122)
  3. Patience is one of the attributes of the Prophets. «كلّ من الصّابرین»(123)
  4. Patience is the key to Paradise. «ام حسبتم ان تدخلوا الجنة و لمّا یأتكم ...»(124)
  5. Patience during divine trials and tests is the touchstone and criterion for recognizing and revealing the face of the strivers (Mujāhidīn) and the patient ones (Sābirīn). «و لنبلونّكم حتّى نعلم المجاهدین منكم والصابرین»(125)
  6. Patience is the cause for receiving the blessings (Salawāt) of Allah. «اولئك علیهم صلوات من ربّهم»(126) (Perhaps one of the reasons for sending blessings upon the Prophet and his Household is that they were the most patient of people.)
  7. Patience, relative to faith, is like the head to the body. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «الصبر من الایمان كالرأس من البدن»(127)
  8. Patience is the measure of the ranks of the people of Paradise. «سلام علیكم بما صبرتم», «اولئك یجزون الغرفة بما صبروا»(128), «و جزاهم بما صبروا جنّة و حریرا»(129)
  9. Patience has degrees. In a tradition, we read that patience in calamity has 300 degrees, patience in obedience has 600 degrees, and patience in refraining from sin has 900 degrees.(130)
  10. Throughout the entire Quran, only for the patient ones is reward without measure mentioned. «انّما یوفّى الصّابرون اجرهم بغیر حساب»(131)
  11. The Glorious Quran mentions gratitude (Shukr) alongside patience, indicating that difficulties are also blessings. «لكلّ صبّار شكور»(132)
  12. Patience was the testament of Imam Husayn علیه السلام to his son, Imam Sajjad علیه السلام. «یا بنىّ اِصبر على الحقّ و ان كان مرّا»(133)
  13. Sometimes several types of patience are seen in a single action, such as the action of Prophet Abraham in sacrificing Ishmael, which required both patience in obedience and submission, and patience in calamity.
