Messages

  1. Patience is the source of all perfections. «بما صبرتم» Patience is mentioned at the end of eight signs and perfections for the people of understanding (Ulul Albāb), and the angels also consider the patience of the believers as the basis of their greeting.
  2. Greeting with Salām upon entry is the method of the angels. «یدخلون علیهم، سلام علیكم»
  3. Paradise is the "Abode of Peace" (Dār al-Salām), because the angels greet the inhabitants of Paradise from every direction. «من كل باب سلام علیكم»
  4. We must respect those who possess steadfastness and perseverance. «سلام علیكم بماصبرتم»
  5. In the divine system, even the use of words, greetings, and congratulations is based on a foundation and wisdom (faith and action), not flattery or exaggeration. «سلام علیكم بما صبرتم»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يَنقُضُونَ عَهْدَ ٱللَّهِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مِيثَـٰقِهِۦ وَيَقْطَعُونَ مَآ أَمَرَ ٱللَّهُ بِهِۦٓ أَن يُوصَلَ وَيُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۙ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمُ ٱللَّعْنَةُ وَلَهُمْ سُوٓءُ ٱلدَّارِ (۲٥)
But as for those who break Allah’s compact after having pledged it solemnly, and sever what Allah has commanded to be joined, and cause corruption in the earth—it is such on whom the curse will lie, and for them will be the ills of the [ultimate] abode. (25)

Key Points

In contrast to the good and prominent characteristics of Ulul Albab (those of understanding)—their fulfillment of covenants and their connection to what Allah has commanded to be joined—the Quran points to a group that possesses the opposite of these characteristics: they break covenants and sever what has been commanded to be joined. Therefore, in opposition to ‘Uqba ad-Dār (the ultimate good abode), Sū’ ad-Dār (the evil abode) is designated for these individuals.

Corruption (fasād) on earth has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran concerning individuals and actions. Among the individuals identified as examples of corrupters (mufsid), Pharaoh is one: «انه كان من المفسدین» (134). Likewise, actions such as destroying crops and progeny (135), creating discord, and murder are also considered instances of corruption on earth. The Quran (136) considers the punishment for the mufsid fī al-arḍ (corrupter on earth) to be killing, crucifixion, amputation of limbs, or exile. It also counts those who harbor intentions of arrogance and corruption in their hearts as being deprived of Divine favors on the Day of Judgment.

Imam Sajjad عليه السلام advised his son: «ایّاك و مصاحبة القاطع لرحمه، فانّى وجدته ملعوناً فى كتاب اللَّه» (137) (Beware of befriending one who severs kinship, for I have found him cursed in the Book of Allah). Avoid friendship and companionship with those who sever kinship, because I found them cursed in the Quran. «یقطعون... لهم اللعنة»

134) Al-Qasas, 4. 135) Al-Baqarah, 205. 136) Al-Ma’idah, 33. 137) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 74, p. 196.

Messages

1- One of the methods of education and propagation is the method of comparison. «یوفون ... عقبى الدار - ینقضون ... سوءالّدار»

2- The starting point of all deviations is man's separation from Allah. «ینقضون عهداللَّه... یفسدون»

3- Severing kinship (qat‘ ar-rahm) is among the major sins, because the promise of punishment has been given for it. «یقطعون... لهم سوء الدّار»

4- Man's corruption spreads everywhere. «یفسدون فى الارض»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ۚ وَفَرِحُوا۟ بِٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَمَا ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ إِلَّا مَتَـٰعٌ (۲٦)
Allah expands the provision for whomever He wishes, and tightens it. They boast of the life of this world, but compared with the Hereafter the life of this world is but a [trifling] enjoyment. (26)

Key Points

Although in this Ayah, the expansion and constriction of provision (rizq) are attributed to Allah, it is evident that the actions of the Wise God (Allah) proceed from expediency (maslahah) and wisdom (hikmah). Just as some of its causes have been explained in certain verses and narrations. Among them is the presence of sin in people's lives, which causes changes in their provision. As we read in Du'a Kumayl: «اللهم اغفرلى الذنوب التى تغیّر النّعم» O God! Forgive me the sins that alter blessings. Sometimes the difference in provision is due to testing individuals, «ولنبلونّكم بشى ء ... و نقص من الاموال...»(138) We shall certainly test you with a decrease in wealth... Sometimes deprivation is due to depriving others of their rights, similar to the story of the garden that burned in the fire, which Surah Al-Qalam refers to. And sometimes inattention to orphans causes constriction of provision. «كلا بل لاتكرمون الیتیم»(139) What is important is that we neither become rebellious upon the expansion of provision and forget everything, nor become utterly hopeless and despairing upon its constriction, for the Divine system is a wise system based on testing, not based on chance, luck, or fortune.

138) Al-Baqarah, 155. 139) Al-Fajr, 17.

Messages

1- Provision is in the hands of Allah, not achieved through cleverness, hypocrisy, breaking covenants, or severing relationships that might necessitate spending (infaq) and giving. «اللَّه یبسط الرزق»

2- Even small provision is decreed based on a wise measure. (The word) «یقدر» (measures) is used instead of (the word) «یضیق» (constricts).

3- Although the world is meager, it deceives its adherents. «و فرحوا...»

4- The world is small, little, and fleeting; let us not become attached to it. «الا متاع»

Ayah

وَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُضِلُّ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَهْدِىٓ إِلَيْهِ مَنْ أَنَابَ (۲۷)
The faithless say, ‘Why has not some sign been sent down to him from his Lord?’ Say, ‘Indeed Allah leads astray whomever He wishes, and guides to Himself those who turn penitently [to Him] (27)

Key Points

Every Prophet, to prove his claim of prophethood, must have a sign from Allah, which is called a miracle. This miracle is naturally in specific and defined matters, but stubborn individuals who did not intend to accept the truth would demand miracles from the Prophet based on their own whims. Whereas, if they set aside their stubbornness, the Quran itself is the greatest miracle.

Sometimes water and food are harmful to sick people, but this harm is not because there is harm in the water and food, but because of the sickness of these individuals. Yes! Individuals who are spiritually sick, like the physically sick, suffer discomfort in accepting the pure Divine verses, because as soon as they encounter the truth, the spirit of stubbornness is provoked in them, and they reject guidance, and this is the meaning of yaḍill (He leads astray).

A discussion concerning guidance (hidayah) and misguidance (ḍalālah). Divine guidance is of two types: initial and complementary. Initial guidance relates to all people, "انّا هدیناه السّبیل" (140). But complementary guidance is for those who have accepted the first general guidance. Like a teacher who, in the first days of class, presents his material uniformly and equally to all students, but after a while, shows greater favor to His diligent, serious, and hardworking students. "والّذین اهتدوا زادهم هدى" (141).

But truly, concerning the individuals about whom the Quran says: "و ما تأتیهم من آیة من آیات ربّهم الا كانوا عنها معرضین" (142) (No verse from the signs of their Lord came to them except that they turned away from it). "و لو نزلنا علیك كتابا فى قرطاس فلمسوه بایدیهم لقال الّذین كفروا ان هذا الا سحر مبین" (143) (Even if We had sent down to you a book on parchment, and they had touched it with their hands, those who disbelieved would still say, 'This is nothing but obvious magic'). "و انْ یروا كلّ آیة لایؤمنوا بها" (144) (If they see every sign, they will not believe in it). Is there any solution other than withdrawing the Hand of favor and sealing their hearts with misguidance?!

In any case, Allah is Wise and Just, and all His actions and Will—from: "یهدى من یشاء" (He guides whom He wills), "یضّل من یشاء" (He leads astray whom He wills), "یرزق من یشاء" (He provides for whom He wills), "یغفر لمن یشاء" (He forgives whom He wills), "یعذّب من یشاء" (He punishes whom He wills), "یخلق ما یشاء" (He creates what He wills)—are all interpreted based on justice, wisdom, grace, and favor. Meaning, if He said: "یهدى من یشاء," it is not that He wills guidance for someone without any rule or criterion; rather, as is evident from other verses, in one place He considers faith a condition for guidance and says: "و من یؤمن باللَّه یهد قلبه" (145) (And whoever believes in Allah, He guides his heart). And in another place, He introduces attaining the satisfaction and pleasure of the Truth (Allah) by traversing the paths of safety as the basis for guidance: "یهدى به اللَّه من اتبع رضوانه سبل السّلام" (146). Or if He said: "یضل من یشاء," in another verse, He considers extravagance, doubt, and suspicion to be the cause of this misguidance. "یضل اللَّه من هو مسرفٌ مرتاب" (147).

In any case, if the mouth of a vessel is facing the sky, rain will enter it, but if it is placed facing the ground, it will not benefit from the heavenly downpour. And such is the human being whose spiritual opening is directed towards materialism; it is obvious that he will be deprived of the rain of Divine spirituality. "استحبوا الحیاة الدنیا على الاخرة و ان اللَّه لایهدى القوم الكافرین" (148).

140) Al-Insan, 3. 141) Muhammad, 17. 142) Al-An'am, 4. 143) Al-An'am, 7. 144) Al-An'am, 25. 145) At-Taghabun, 11. 146) Al-Ma'idah, 16. 147) Ghafir, 34. 148) An-Nahl, 107.

Messages

  1. Stubborn disbelievers demand a new miracle every moment. The problem is not with the external miracle, but with their internal obstinacy. "لولا اُنزل علیه آیة"
  2. The Divine tradition is the guidance of all people, "انّ علینا للهُدى" (149). But if someone chooses the crooked path, Allah makes his punishment that very misguidance. "یضل من یشاء"
  3. Humility before the Truth is the key to guidance. "یهدى الیه من اناب"
  4. Although the guidance of people is in Allah's Hand, His guidance includes those who have created readiness within themselves. "یهدى من اناب"

149) Al-Layl, 12.

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَتَطْمَئِنُّ قُلُوبُهُم بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ أَلَا بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ تَطْمَئِنُّ ٱلْقُلُوبُ (۲۸)
—those who have faith and whose hearts find rest in the remembrance of Allah.’ Behold! The hearts find rest in Allah’s remembrance! (28)

Key Points

The remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah is not limited to verbal mention, although it is one of the clear manifestations of the remembrance of Allah, because what is important is being mindful of Allah in all states, especially at the time of sin.

The remembrance of Allah has many blessings, including: A: Remembering His blessings is a factor for thanking Him. B: Remembering His power is the cause of reliance (Tawakkul) upon Him. C: Remembering His kindness is the source of love for Him. D: Remembering His might and wrath is a factor for fearing Him. E: Remembering His greatness and majesty is the cause of awe (Khushyat) before Him. F: Remembering His knowledge of the hidden and the manifest is the source of our modesty (Haya) and chastity. G: Remembering His pardon and generosity is the source of hope and repentance (Tawbah). H: Remembering His justice is a factor for piety (Taqwa) and abstinence.

Man seeks the infinite and desires absolute perfection, but since everything other than Allah is limited and has an accidental existence, it does not bring peace to the heart.

In contrast to those who find peace through the remembrance of Allah, some people are satisfied with the meager provisions of the world. «رضوا بالحیاة الدنیا واطمأنوا بها»

Prayer (Salat) is divine remembrance (Dhikr Ilahi) and a source of peace. «و اقم الصّلاة لذكرى»(150), «الا بذكر اللَّه تطمئن»

It is possible that the meaning of «الا بذكر اللَّه تطمئن القلوب» is that your hearts find peace through the remembrance and mention that Allah makes of you. That is, if we know that Allah remembers us and that we are in His presence, our hearts find tranquility. Just as Prophet Noah عليه السلام found peace through the divine words «اصنع الفلك باعیننا»(151), and Imam Husayn عليه السلام expressed this peace at the time of the martyrdom of his son Ali Asghar with the phrase «هیّن علىّ انّه بعین اللَّه», or, as stated in the Dua of Arafah: «یا ذاكر الذاكرین».

Question

Although it is stated in this Ayah that hearts, especially the heart of the believer, find peace through the remembrance of Allah, in other verses we read that whenever the believer remembers Allah, his heart trembles. «انما المؤمنون الّذین اذا ذكر اللَّه وجلت قلوبهم»(152) Can this trembling and peace be combined in one place? What is the way to reconcile this?

Answer

To conceive of the simultaneous existence of this peace and trembling in one person, attention to the following examples is helpful: A: Sometimes a person has confidence and peace due to the existence of all prerequisites, but at the same time, he is worried and fearful of the outcome. Like a specialist surgeon who is confident in his knowledge and work, but still feels anxiety when operating on an important personality. B: Children both feel peace in the presence of their parents and hold them in awe and fear them. C: Sometimes a person is pleased and calm because he knows that a certain misfortune is for his trial, growth, and elevation of status, but the question of whether he will succeed in fulfilling his duty or not makes him worried and causes him to tremble. D: When believers recite the Quran and reach the verses of punishment, Hellfire, and divine wrath, trembling overcomes their bodies, but when they recite the verses of Allah's mercy, pleasure, and Paradise, a sweet peace envelops their hearts and reassures them.

Imam Sajjad عليه السلام says in the Dua of Abu Hamzah: «اذا ذكرت ذنوبى فزعت و اذا رأیت كرمك طمعت», meaning whenever I remember my sins and Your justice and wrath, I cry out, but when I remember Your kindness and pardon, I become hopeful.

The author of Al-Mizan brings evidence for this statement from the Quran: «تقشعر منه جلود الّذین یخشون ربّهم ثم تلین جلودهم و قلوبهم الى ذكر اللَّه»(153). Meaning, initially, a person feels anxiety, but gradually he reaches peace.

Whoever is heedless of the remembrance of Allah has no peace, and a life without peace is a miserable life. «من اعرض عن ذكرى فانّ له معیشة ضنكا»(154)

Factors of Peace and Reassurance Achieving confidence and peace can have various factors, but at the head of them, awareness and knowledge have a special manifestation: - Whoever knows that an atom's weight of his work is accounted for, «مثقال ذرة خیراً یره»(155), is reassured regarding his effort and activity. - Whoever knows that he was created based on divine grace and mercy, «الاّ من رحم ربّك و لذلك خلقهم»(156), is hopeful. - Whoever knows that Allah is watching the oppressors, «انّ ربّك لبالمرصاد»(157), has peace. - Whoever knows that Allah is Wise (Hakim) and All-Knowing (Alim) and has not created any being in vain «علیم حكیم» is optimistic. - Whoever knows that his path is clear and his future is better than his past, «والاخرة خیر وابقى»(158), his heart is confident. - Whoever knows that his Imam and leader is a perfect human being, chosen by Allah and infallible (Ma'sum) from every slip and error, «انى جاعلك للنّاس اماما»(159), is at peace. - Whoever knows that his good deed is rewarded ten to seven hundred times, or even infinitely, but his evil deed is counted as only one slip, is happy. «مثل الّذین ینفقون اموالهم فى سبیل اللَّه كمثل حبة انبتت سبع سنابل فى كلّ سنبلة مأئة حبة»(160) - Whoever knows that Allah loves the doers of good, «ان اللَّه یحب المحسنین»(161), is reassured by his good work. - Whoever knows that his good deed is revealed and his evil deed is concealed, «یا من اظهر الجمیل و ستر القبیح», is joyful.

Factors of Anxiety and Worry One of the most common diseases of the present century is anxiety and depression. Many reasons have been cited for this disease, which has symptoms such as isolationism, introspection, low self-esteem, and feeling useless, including: - The depressed person becomes depressed because he does not see everything according to his own desire, whereas we should not give up and surrender the possible amount just because we did not achieve everything we wanted. - The depressed person thinks to himself why everyone does not love him, while this is impossible, and even Allah and Gabriel have enemies; therefore, man should not expect everyone to love him. - The depressed person assumes that all people are bad, whereas this is not the case, and Allah answered the angels who had this illusion. - The depressed person assumes that all misfortunes come from outside his being, whereas the majority of bitterness is the reaction and response to our own characteristics and deeds. - The anxious person is worried about starting tasks and feels fear and loneliness. Imam Ali عليه السلام says to alleviate this state: «اذاخفت من شى ءٍ فقع فیه»(162). Throw yourself into whatever you fear, for the fear of anything is greater than the thing itself. - The anxious person worries about what the future holds. This state can be treated with reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul) and perseverance. - Because he has failed in some tasks, he worries that he might suffer this fate in all matters. - Because he relies on unstable individuals and powers, he becomes anxious when they falter. - In summary, matters such as people's lack of appreciation for their efforts, sin, fear of death, family suggestions that 'you don't know' and 'you can't,' hasty judgments, undue expectations, and wrong perceptions are the cause of much depression and anxiety, which can be transformed into peace and vitality through the remembrance of Allah and His power, pardon, and grace.

I am writing the commentary on the Ayah «الّذین آمنوا و تطمئنّ قلوبهم بذكر اللَّه» on the 14th of Khordad, 1373 (Solar Hijri calendar), in Hamadan, because I have come to speak on the occasion of the fifth anniversary of the passing of Imam Khomeini (ره). The one who, at the end of his very important will, says: «من با دلى آرام و قلبى مطمئن و ضمیرى شاد و روحى امیدوار به فضل الهى از خدمت شما مرخص مى شوم.» (I depart from your service with a tranquil heart, a confident soul, a cheerful conscience, and a spirit hopeful of divine grace.)

150) Taha, 14. 151) Hud, 37. 152) Anfal, 2. 153) Zumar, 23. 154) Taha, 124. 155) Zalzalah, 7. 156) Hud, 119. 157) Fajr, 14. 158) Al-A'la, 17. 159) Baqarah, 124. 160) Baqarah, 261. 161) Baqarah, 195. 162) Bihar, Vol. 71, p. 362.

Messages

1- The sign of true turning (Inabah) is faith and confidence in Allah. «من اناب، الذین امنوا ...»

2- Faith without heartfelt confidence is neither complete nor effective. «امنوا وتطمئن قلوبهم»

3- Verbal remembrance of Allah is not sufficient; it also requires heartfelt confidence. «تطمئن قلوبهم بذكر اللَّه»

4- Only the remembrance of Allah becomes the source of peace for the heart. «بذكر اللَّه تطمئن القلوب» Today, those who possess wealth, power, and deceit are numerous, but there is no sign of the necessary peace.

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ طُوبَىٰ لَهُمْ وَحُسْنُ مَـَٔابٍ (۲۹)
Those who have faith and do righteous deeds—happy are they and good is their [ultimate] destination. (29)

Key Points

People are four categories: A: The Believer (Mu'min), those who possess both faith (Iman) and righteous deeds (Salih). B: The Disbeliever (Kafir), those who lack faith and do not outwardly express belief. C: The Transgressor (Fasiq), those who possess faith but whose deeds are not righteous. D: The Hypocrite (Munafiq), those who lack faith but outwardly express belief.

Tuba (طوبى) is either a verbal noun (masdar) like bushra (بُشرى), or it is the feminine form of atyab (اطیب), meaning "the best." In the Ayah, a specific instance for "(the best)" is not mentioned, so that it may encompass all best things. And perhaps the narrations which state that Tuba is a tree whose root is in the house of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and Ali عليه السلام, and whose branches cast shade upon the believers (163), are an example and embodiment of the fact that all purity and goodness depend on connection to the heavenly leaders.

When the narrow-minded criticized the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, asking why He kissed Fatimah al-Zahra عليها السلام so much, He replied: When I was taken to Paradise on the Night of Mi'raj (Ascension), I ate from the fruit of the Tuba tree, and the existence of Fatimah originated and came into being from it. And whenever I long for the scent of Paradise, I smell my daughter Fatimah. (164)

The success (or enjoyment) of faithless individuals who are far from righteous deeds lacks depth and continuity. As Imam Ali عليه السلام states: «لاخیر فى لذّة من بعدها النّار» (165) There is no good in a pleasure after which there is the Fire.

163) Bihar, Vol. 8, pp. 117 and 120. 164) Bihar, Vol. 8, p. 188. 165) Bihar, Vol. 41, p. 104.

Messages

1- Sweet worldly life and a good outcome in the Hereafter are achieved under the shade of faith and righteous deeds. «الذین آمنوا... طوبى لهم و حسن ماب»

2- Worldly successes are valuable only if they are accompanied by an outcome in the Hereafter, and not if they become an obstacle to it. «طوبى لهم و حسن ماب»

Ayah

كَذَٰلِكَ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ فِىٓ أُمَّةٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهَآ أُمَمٌ لِّتَتْلُوَا۟ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ وَهُمْ يَكْفُرُونَ بِٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ۚ قُلْ هُوَ رَبِّى لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَإِلَيْهِ مَتَابِ (۳٠)
Thus have We sent you to a nation before which many nations have passed away, that you may recite to them what We have revealed to you. Yet they defy the All-beneficent. Say, ‘He is my Lord; there is no god except Him; in Him I have put my trust, and to Him will be my return.’ (30)

Key Points

Although the appointment (Bi'that) of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was among the Arab nation, according to the explicit statement of other verses, He is the Prophet for all nations. «وما ارسلناك الا كافة للناس»(166)

166) Saba, 28.

Messages

1- The appointment of the prophets is a Divine tradition and based on His grace and mercy. «كذلك...بالرحمن»

2- Attention to the history of every nation is necessary for their propagation and education. «قد خلت من قبلها»

3- The duty of the prophets is the recitation of the Divine revelation upon the people. «لتتلوَا»

5- Resist the disbelievers (Kuffar) and defend your belief. «یكفرون بالرحمن، قل هو ربّى»

6- Reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul) is the factor for resistance against the disbelievers. «قل هو ربّى علیه توكلّت»

7- The reasons for our faith in Allah are: His Lordship (Rububiyyah) «ربّى», His Oneness «لااله الاهو», His being the refuge «علیه توكلت», and our return to Him «الیه متاب».

Ayah

وَلَوْ أَنَّ قُرْءَانًا سُيِّرَتْ بِهِ ٱلْجِبَالُ أَوْ قُطِّعَتْ بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضُ أَوْ كُلِّمَ بِهِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ ۗ بَل لِّلَّهِ ٱلْأَمْرُ جَمِيعًا ۗ أَفَلَمْ يَا۟يْـَٔسِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَن لَّوْ يَشَآءُ ٱللَّهُ لَهَدَى ٱلنَّاسَ جَمِيعًا ۗ وَلَا يَزَالُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ تُصِيبُهُم بِمَا صَنَعُوا۟ قَارِعَةٌ أَوْ تَحُلُّ قَرِيبًا مِّن دَارِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ يَأْتِىَ وَعْدُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُخْلِفُ ٱلْمِيعَادَ (۳۱)
If only it were a Quran whereby the mountains could be moved, or the earth could be toured, or the dead could be spoken to.... Indeed, all dispensation belongs to Allah. Have not the faithful yet realised that had Allah wished He would have guided mankind all together? The faithless will continue to be visited by catastrophes because of their doings—or they will land near their habitations—until Allah’s promise comes to pass. Indeed Allah does not break His promise. (31)

Key Points

This Ayah expresses the peak of the obstinacy and stubbornness of the disbelievers. Similar to Ayah 111 of Surah Al-An'am, which states: If We had sent down the angels to them, and the dead had spoken to them, and everything had been gathered before them, they still would not believe. The Quran is a book that shook things firmer than mountains and revived things greater than physical dead bodies. It gave life to the dead and stony minds and hearts of the ignorant Arabs. And if there is a book that can shake mountains and revive the dead, it is this very Quran. «او من كان میتاً فاحییناه...»(167) It is understood from the statement of Imam Kadhim عليه السلام that the Quran contains sciences and secrets through which one can influence nature.(168) «سُیّرت به، قُطّعت به، كُلّم به»

167) Tafsir Safi. 168) Tafsir Kanz ad-Daqa'iq.

Messages

1- Do not expect all people to believe. «لو یشاءاللَّه لهدى الناس جمیعاً»

2- Obstinacy and stubbornness are the incurable ailments of human societies. (If someone is seeking and pursuing the truth, even one miracle is enough for him to believe.) «قرآناً سُیّرت به، قطّعت به، كلّم به»

3- In the divine worldview, Allah is the ultimate Doer of all things. «بل للَّه الامر جمیعاً»

4- Miracles are subject to the Will of Allah, not based on the suggestions or whims of stubborn individuals. «بل للَّه الامر جمیعاً»

5- The Will of Allah is for the voluntary guidance of humans, not their compulsion or coercion. «لو یشاء اللَّه لهدى الناس جمیعاً» Just as He says in another Ayah: «لا اكراه فى الدین»(169)

6- The disbelievers must await blows from Allah upon their country and borders. «لایزال الّذین كفروا تصیبهم بما صنعوا قارعه»

7- The wrath of Allah is not exclusive to the Hereafter. «تصیبهم ... قارعه»

8- Admonition and reasoning are not always sufficient and effective; sometimes wrath and punishment are necessary. «تصیبهم ... قارعه»

9- The cause of calamities descending is our own deeds. «بما صنعوا قارعه»

10- Divine warnings are sometimes direct, concerning ourselves, and sometimes indirect, concerning others and the regions around us. «تحلّ قریباً من دارهم»

11- We should learn lessons from the punishment of others. «او تحّل قریباً من دارهم»

12- Never doubt the promises and warnings of Allah. «لا یخلف المیعاد»

169) Baqarah, 256.

Ayah

وَلَقَدِ ٱسْتُهْزِئَ بِرُسُلٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَأَمْلَيْتُ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُهُمْ ۖ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ عِقَابِ (۳۲)
Apostles were certainly derided before you. But then I gave respite to those who were faithless, then I seized them; so how was My retribution? (32)

Messages

1- Knowing the history of the prophets is a factor that leads to patience and forbearance when facing problems and enduring hardships and calamities. «استهزء برسل من قبلك»

2- Whoever mocks the divine messengers is a disbeliever (Kafir). «فاملیت للّذین كفروا»

3- Granting respite is a definite and eternal divine tradition (Sunnah). «فَاَملیت» (The believer utilizes this opportunity for repentance and righteous deeds, but the disbeliever persists in his sins.)

4- Let us not be deceived by the divine respite, for His wrath arrives suddenly. «ثُمّ اخذتهم»

5- If you commission someone to perform a task, warn him of the dangers and hardships of the work and give him encouragement. «لقد استهزء برسل من قبلك»

Ayah

أَفَمَنْ هُوَ قَآئِمٌ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ نَفْسٍۭ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ ۗ وَجَعَلُوا۟ لِلَّهِ شُرَكَآءَ قُلْ سَمُّوهُمْ ۚ أَمْ تُنَبِّـُٔونَهُۥ بِمَا لَا يَعْلَمُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَم بِظَـٰهِرٍ مِّنَ ٱلْقَوْلِ ۗ بَلْ زُيِّنَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مَكْرُهُمْ وَصُدُّوا۟ عَنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ ۗ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِنْ هَادٍ (۳۳)
Is He who sustains every soul in spite of what it earns [comparable to the idols]? Yet they ascribe partners to Allah! Say, ‘Name them!’ Will you inform Him concerning something He does not know about on the earth, or concerning [what are] mere words? Indeed, to the faithless, their scheming is presented as decorous, and they have been barred from the [right] way; and whomever Allah leads astray, has no guide. (33)

Key Points

Allah being the Maintainer (al-Qayyūm) means His management of all affairs, sufficiency, protection, supervision, and the recording and regulation of them.

Messages

1- By posing a question, Allah calls upon human intellect and innate nature (fitrah) to judge. This is because intellect and innate nature are incompatible with polytheism (shirk). «أفمن... ام تنبئونه»

2- Whoever separates himself from Allah, the All-Knowing (Alīm), the One (Wāhid), and the Maintainer (Qayyūm), falls into the abyss of polytheism (shirk) and multiple deities. «شركاء»

3- Idol worship and polytheism (shirk) are so frivolous and illogical that one cannot even state the names and attributes of their deities. «قل سمّوهم»

4- Perhaps the polytheists themselves do not truly accept the idols deep down. «ام بظاهر من القول»

5- Polytheism (shirk) is also a type of disbelief (kufr). «جعلوا اللَّه شركاء... زیّن للذین كفروا»

6- Whoever finds deviation appealing is prevented from the path of truth and the way of guidance. «زُیّن... صدّوا»

7- Do not be deceived by your own schemes and plans. «زیّن... مكرهم»

Ayah

لَّهُمْ عَذَابٌ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَشَقُّ ۖ وَمَا لَهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ مِن وَاقٍ (۳٤)
There is a punishment for them in the life of this world, and the punishment of the Hereafter will surely be harder, and they have no defender against Allah. (34)

Key Points

The punishment of the Hereafter is harder, because: A: In the Resurrection, means and connections are severed. «تقطّعت بهم الاسباب»(170) B: Kinship and family ties are no longer effective. «فلاانساب بینهم یومئذ»(171) C: Ransom is not accepted. «یودّ المجرم لویفتدى... ببنیه وصاحبته و اخیه...و من فى الارض جمیعا كلاّ»(172) He is willing to sacrifice relatives and even the entire world. D: Apology is useless. «یوم لاینفع الظالمین معذرتهم»(173) E: Friends abandon one another. «لایسئل حمیم حمیما»(174) F: It is lasting and eternal. «خالدین فیها»(175) G: There is no alleviation. «لایخفّف»(176) H: It is both spiritual and physical. «ذق انّك انت العزیز الكریم»(177)

170) Baqarah, 166. 171) Mu’minun, 101. 172) Ma’arij, 11 to 16. 173) Ghafir, 52. 174) Ma’arij, 10. 175) Baqarah, 162. 176) Baqarah, 162. 177) Dukhan, 49.

Messages

1- Whoever sets up partners for Allah is also punished in this world. «لهم عذاب فى... الدنیا»

2- Intercession does not include polytheists. «ما لهم من اللَّه من واقٍ»

Ayah

۞ مَّثَلُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ٱلَّتِى وُعِدَ ٱلْمُتَّقُونَ ۖ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۖ أُكُلُهَا دَآئِمٌ وَظِلُّهَا ۚ تِلْكَ عُقْبَى ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوا۟ ۖ وَّعُقْبَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ ٱلنَّارُ (۳٥)
A description of the paradise promised to the Godwary: streams run in it, its fruits and shade are everlasting. Such is the requital of those who are Godwary; and the requital of the faithless is the Fire. (35)

Key Points

Among the methods of teaching and nurturing in the Quran is comparison and assessment between individuals, states, and the ultimate outcome of truth and falsehood. For this reason, since the future of the polytheists was mentioned in the previous Ayah, He therefore refers in this Ayah to the destiny of the pious individuals, so that people, by comparing these two destinies, can choose their path with greater and better understanding and awareness.

Messages

  1. What can be understood about Paradise is only its likeness or example, «مَثَل الجنّة», otherwise, Paradise is not truly comprehensible for a limited existence like ours.

  2. The blessings of Paradise are not seasonal, localized, or perishable; interruption, cessation, or affliction have no way into it. «دائم» (permanent).

  3. Paradise is the price of piety (Taqwa) and is not given to anyone without reason. The reward for temporary worldly abstinence is permanent otherworldly fulfillment. «الجنة التى وعد المتقون...اكلها دائم»

  4. Do not look at the outward appearance of individuals, because the end result is what matters. «عقبى الذین اتقوا»

  5. A believer must possess piety (Taqwa). In contrast to «المتقون» (the righteous), «الكافرین» (the disbelievers) is mentioned.

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ يَفْرَحُونَ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ ۖ وَمِنَ ٱلْأَحْزَابِ مَن يُنكِرُ بَعْضَهُۥ ۚ قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ ٱللَّهَ وَلَآ أُشْرِكَ بِهِۦٓ ۚ إِلَيْهِ أَدْعُوا۟ وَإِلَيْهِ مَـَٔابِ (۳٦)
Those whom We have given the Book rejoice in what has been sent down to you. Some of the factions deny a part of it. Say, ‘Indeed I have been commanded to worship Allah and not to ascribe any partner to Him. To Him do I summon [all mankind] and to Him will be my return.’ (36)

Key Points

This Ayah, like other Ayahs (178), praises the sound, good-hearted, and truth-accepting individuals among the People of the Book (Ahl al-Kitab) and states: The true People of the Book possess a truth-seeking spirit; they avoid undue prejudices, and when they encounter Islam and the Divine verses, they become joyful and embrace faith.

(178) Such as: Al-Qasas 52, Al-Baqarah 120, and Al-A'raf 159.

Messages

1- Although accepting the verses is important, joyful acceptance is more important. «یفرحون»

2- Faith in all Divine commands is necessary, not just a part of them. «بما انزل»

3- Blind partisanship is a blight and a danger against truth-seeking. «من الاحزاب من ینكر»

4- Firmness must be shown in the face of the denial of truth. «من ینكر... قُل»

5- The reason for sincere servitude is that our destiny and future are in His hands. «اعبداللَّه... الیه مآب»

6- Monotheism (Tawhid), Prophethood (Nubuwwah), and the Resurrection (Ma'ad) are the common principles of all heavenly religions;

Monotheism, «اعبداللَّه ولا اشرك به» Prophethood, «الیه ادعوا» Resurrection, «الیه مآب»

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ حُكْمًا عَرَبِيًّا ۚ وَلَئِنِ ٱتَّبَعْتَ أَهْوَآءَهُم بَعْدَ مَا جَآءَكَ مِنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ مَا لَكَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا وَاقٍ (۳۷)
Thus We have sent it down as a dispensation in Arabic; and should you follow their desires after the knowledge that has come to you, you shall have against Allah neither any guardian nor defender. (37)

Key Points

The word «حكم» (hukm) in the Ayah refers to one of three meanings: the ruler and separator between truth and falsehood, divine decrees, or wisdom.

Messages

1- The sending of the prophets and the revelation of heavenly books is a wise divine tradition. «و كذلك»

2- The message of revelation must be in the language of the people. «حكماً عربیاً»

3- Divine commands are clear, without ambiguity or deviation. «عربیّاً»

4- More contemptible than following one's own desires and lust is catering to the desires of others. «اهوائهم»

5- Knowledge alone does not bring happiness; it is possible that a person, despite having knowledge, still follows the superstitions and desires of the people. «اتّبعت اهوائهم بعد ما جاءك من العلم»

6- Aware people have greater responsibility. «جاءك من العلم»

7- True knowledge is the Quran. «انزلناه... جاءك من العلم»

8- Allah has no kinship with anyone; even if the prophets deviate, they will be subject to divine chastisement. «مالك من اللَّه»

9- Seeking the satisfaction of the deviated is synonymous with losing divine aid. «اتّبعت اهواءهم... ما لك من اللَّه»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا رُسُلًا مِّن قَبْلِكَ وَجَعَلْنَا لَهُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا وَذُرِّيَّةً ۚ وَمَا كَانَ لِرَسُولٍ أَن يَأْتِىَ بِـَٔايَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ لِكُلِّ أَجَلٍ كِتَابٌ (۳۸)
Certainly We have sent apostles before you, and We appointed for them wives and descendants; and an apostle may not bring a sign except by Allah’s leave. There is a written [ordinance] for every term: (38)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: We are the children of the Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, and our mother is Fatimah عليها السلام. Allah has not given anything to any of the Prophets except that He has also given it to the Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. Then, that Imam recited this Ayah: (179) «ولقد ارسلنا رسلا...»

179) Bihar, Vol. 24, p. 265.

Messages

1- The sending of the Prophets (Bi'that) is one of the Divine traditions. «ارسلنا رسلاً»

2- Prophets, like other people, lived normal lives and had wives and children. «ازواجاً و ذریّة»

3- The miracle of the Prophets is not based on their own desire or the request and whim of the people, but rather by the permission and will of Allah. «الاّ باذنه»

4- Time plays a role in the revelation of Divine law. «لكلّ اجل كتاب»

Ayah

يَمْحُوا۟ ٱللَّهُ مَا يَشَآءُ وَيُثْبِتُ ۖ وَعِندَهُۥٓ أُمُّ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ (۳۹)
Allah effaces and confirms whatever He wishes and with Him is the Mother Book. (39)

Key Points

A discussion concerning Divine Decrees (Qadar). According to what is derived from the verses (Ayat) and narrations (Riwayat), Divine Decrees are of two types:

  1. Matters that have a permanent expediency (Maslahah) and thus their law is also permanent. Such as the verses: «ما یبدّل القول لدىّ»(180) (There is no change in Our Word). Change has no way into Our speech. «كلّ شى ءٍ عنده بمقدار»(181) (Everything with the Lord is measured precisely). Everything with the Lord has a precise calculation and measure. Decrees of this kind are recorded in the Preserved Tablet: «فى لوح محفوظ»(182), and only those close to Allah (the Muqarrabun) can become aware of it with His permission. «كتاب مرقوم یشهده المقرّبون»(183).
  2. Matters that are non-absolute (non-deterministic), and their expediencies depend on the actions and behavior of people, such as people repenting from sin, which entails the expediency of forgiveness; or giving charity (Sadaqah), which brings with it the expediency of averting calamity; or oppression and tyranny, which, due to their corruption (Mafsadah), lead to Divine Wrath. Meaning, the Almighty Allah is not constrained in administering the system of creation, and with the infinite wisdom and knowledge that He possesses, He can introduce changes in creation and its laws by changing the conditions. It is obvious that these changes are not a sign of ignorance on the part of the Almighty Allah, nor are they a revision or regret on His part. Rather, these changes are based on wisdom, the alteration of conditions, or the conclusion of the period designated for that matter. The Glorious Quran has many examples in this regard, a few of which we mention: A: «اُدعونى استجب لكم»(184) (Call upon Me, I will respond to you). Through supplication and humility, man can achieve his expediencies and change his destiny. B: «لعلّ اللَّه یحدث بعد ذلك امرا»(185) (Perhaps Allah will bring about a new matter after that). The Divine law is not fixed everywhere; perhaps Allah will bring about a new plan when the necessary conditions arise. C: «كلّ یوم هو فى شأن»(186) (Every day He is engaged in some affair). Allah is engaged every day in the affair specific to that day. D: «فلّما زاغوا، ازاغ اللَّه»(187) (So when they deviated, Allah caused their hearts to deviate). Since they chose the path of deviation, Allah also caused them to deviate. E: «و لو انّ اهل القرى آمنوا واتقوا لفتحنا علیهم بركات»(188) (If the people of the towns had believed and been righteous, We would have opened upon them blessings). Through faith and piety (Taqwa), the path of Divine Wrath changes to grace and blessing. F: «ان اللَّه لایغیّر مابقوم حتّى یغیّروا ما بانفسهم»(189) (Indeed, Allah does not change the condition of a people until they change what is in themselves). Allah does not change the destiny of any people unless they change themselves. G: «الاّ مَن تاب و آمن و عمل عملا صالحاً فاولئك یبدّل اللَّه سیئاتهم حسنات»(190) (Except for those who repent, believe and do righteous work. For them, Allah will change their evil deeds into good deeds). Those who repent, believe, and perform good deeds—Allah transforms their evil deeds into good deeds (Hasanat). H: «ان عُدتم عُدنا»(191) (If you return [to sin], We will return [to punishment]). If you return, We will also return.

Question: If Allah’s knowledge is identical to His Essence and is unchangeable, then everything that has passed in His knowledge must come to fruition, otherwise it would imply ignorance.

Answer: Allah’s knowledge is based on the system of causes and effects (Al-Ilal wa Al-Asbab). In this way, He knows that if this means is used, this result will follow, and if another means is utilized, a different outcome will ensue. His knowledge is not separate from the knowledge of the causes and effects.

What is Bada'?

One of the objections raised by the Ahl al-Sunnah against the Shi'a is that they claim the Shi'a attribute Bada' (Divine Alteration/Manifestation) to Allah. They assume Bada' means a change in Divine knowledge or the discovery of a contrary fact by Allah. However, what the Shi'a mean by Bada' is the manifestation of something that we humans had assumed to be otherwise.

Bada' in creation is similar to Naskh (abrogation) in law. For example, we might assume from the apparent meaning of a law or ruling that it is permanent, but after a period, we see that it has changed. Of course, this does not mean regret or ignorance on the part of the Lawgiver. Rather, conditions have caused this change in the law, just like a prescription written by a doctor based on the patient's current condition, but as soon as the patient's state changes, the doctor prescribes a new one. Therefore, just as all Islamic sects, both Shi'a and Sunni, have accepted Naskh (abrogation) in the verses—which is essentially a type of Bada'—they must also accept Bada' with the meaning and explanation provided here.

Thus, Bada' signifies our ignorance, not the ignorance of Allah.

Examples of Bada':

  1. We assumed that when Allah commanded Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) عليه السلام to sacrifice his son, He intended for Isma'il عليه السلام to be killed and his blood spilled on the ground. But later, it became manifest that the Divine Will behind this command was the testing of the father, not the killing of the son.
  2. We assumed from Allah's promise to Prophet Musa (Moses) عليه السلام that the period of intimate communion (Munajat) was thirty nights: «و واعدنا موسى ثلاثین لیلة»(192). But later, it became manifest that the plan was forty nights from the beginning, but Allah announced it in two stages for the purpose of testing: first thirty nights, and then ten nights.
  3. We thought that the Qibla (direction of prayer) for Muslims was permanently the Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem), but the verses concerning the change of Qibla made it manifest to us that the permanent Qibla was the Ka'bah.
  4. When the signs of Allah's Wrath appeared, even Prophet Yunus (Jonah) عليه السلام was certain that the Divine punishment would descend and his disbelieving people would be destroyed. Thus, he left the people. However, the people believed, and the Divine Wrath was averted. «الاّ قوم یونس لمّا آمنوا كشفنا عنهم»(193).

In any case, the meaning of Bada' is not the ignorance of Allah or the changing of His knowledge. This is because Allah knew from the beginning that Isma'il's blood would not be spilled, that the duration of Musa's communion was forty nights, that the permanent Qibla of the Muslims would be the Ka'bah, and that the people of Yunus were destined for salvation. However, the apparent instructions and events were such that man had a different perception. Therefore, no change has occurred in Allah’s knowledge; it is we who have gained a new perspective.

Bada' in this sense has many educational effects, including that man remains hopeful for a change in conditions until the last moment of his life, the spirit of reliance (Tawakkul) is revived in him, he is not captivated by appearances, and his faith in the Unseen and the power of the Almighty Allah increases. Through repentance, charity, intimate communion, and supplication, he strives to change the course of events and Divine Wrath.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Allah took a covenant from all the Prophets, along with Monotheism (Tawhid), regarding faith in Bada'.

In another Hadith, we read: Whoever assumes that a new matter has become clear to Allah which He did not know before, disassociate yourselves from him.(194)

180) Qaf, 29. 181) Al-Baqarah, 162. 182) Al-Baqarah, 162. 183) Al-Mutaffifin, 20 - 21. 184) Ghafir, 60. 185) At-Talaq, 1. 186) Ar-Rahman, 29. 187) As-Saff, 5. 188) Al-A'raf, 69. 189) Ar-Ra'd, 11. 190) Al-Furqan, 70. 191) Al-Isra, 8. 192) Al-A'raf, 142. 193) Yunus, 98. 194) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Allah’s Hand is open for change in the system of creation (Takwin) or legislation (Tashri'). «یمحوا اللَّه مایشاء»

2- Allah has not abandoned creation to its own devices. «یمحوا اللَّه مایشاء ویثبت»

3- The effacement or confirmation of the laws governing the universe is in Allah’s Hand. «یمحوا اللَّه...»

4- Divine effacement and confirmation are based on wisdom and knowledge. «و عنده ام الكتاب»

5- The world of creation has a register in which all events are recorded. «الكتاب»

Ayah

وَإِن مَّا نُرِيَنَّكَ بَعْضَ ٱلَّذِى نَعِدُهُمْ أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ وَعَلَيْنَا ٱلْحِسَابُ (٤٠)
Whether We show you a part of what We promise them, or take you away [before that], your duty is only to communicate, and it is for Us to do the reckoning. (40)

Messages

1- What is observed of Allah's favor upon the Muslims or His divine wrath upon the disbelievers is only a part of the fulfillment of the divine promises. «بعض الذى نعدهم»

2- Even the death of individuals like the noble Messenger of Islam does not prevent the fulfillment of the divine promises, and they will be realized at the foreseen time. «نرینّك... او نتوفینّك»

3- Sometimes the effects of propagation appear after the propagator has departed, so when performing a duty, we should not await immediate results. Because delay is not a sign of cessation. «...او نتوفینّك»

4- Divine punishments are not without reckoning, misplaced, or excessive. «علینا الحساب»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّا نَأْتِى ٱلْأَرْضَ نَنقُصُهَا مِنْ أَطْرَافِهَا ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ لَا مُعَقِّبَ لِحُكْمِهِۦ ۚ وَهُوَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ (٤۱)
Have they not seen how We visit the land diminishing it at its edges? Allah judges, and there is none who may repeal His judgement, and He is swift at reckoning. (41)

Key Points

We read in numerous narrations that the meaning of the diminishing of the sides of the earth is the death and loss of great scholars and learned figures.(195)

195) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. A similar verse is also mentioned in Surah Al-Anbiya, Ayah 44.

Messages

1- We should learn lessons from the history and fate of those who passed away, and we should not doubt the divine promises and warnings. «أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا»

2- The end of the lifespan of governments and personalities is by the Divine Will. «نَأْتِي... نَنْقُصُهَا»

4- The rule and command of Allah are invincible. «لَا مُعَقِّبَ لِحُكْمِهِ»

5- All individuals and powers are gradually removed from the earth, but the power of Allah is stable and enduring. «نَنْقُصُهَا... وَاللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ»

Ayah

وَقَدْ مَكَرَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ فَلِلَّهِ ٱلْمَكْرُ جَمِيعًا ۖ يَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْسِبُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ۗ وَسَيَعْلَمُ ٱلْكُفَّـٰرُ لِمَنْ عُقْبَى ٱلدَّارِ (٤۲)
Those who were before them [also] schemed; yet all devising belongs to Allah. He knows what every soul earns. Soon the faithless will know in whose favour the outcome of that abode will be. (42)

Key Points

The Quran, so that man's entire attention is focused on Allah and he despairs of others, closes all paths that might cause man to incline toward others besides Allah. For example, He says: «انّ العزة للَّه جمیعاً». If you seek others for the sake of gaining might, know that all might belongs to Allah. Elsewhere He says: «انّ القوّة للَّه جمیعاً». All powers are with Allah. And in this Ayah He says: «فللَّه المكر جمیعاً». All planning belongs to Allah, so that man does not rely on others to achieve might, power, and politics. One of the best examples of Allah's planning (Makar) is granting respite to the deviants, in such a way that they imagine their path is correct. But Allah has stated repeatedly in the Quran that the disbelievers should not think that granting respite is a sign of His love for them; rather, We grant respite until their measure is full.

Messages

1- Religious leaders should not worry about the schemes of opponents, because throughout history these tricks have also been used against the Prophets. «قد مكر الذین من قبلهم»

2- Take lessons from history regarding who the ultimate outcome of the abode belongs to. «لمن عقبى الدار»

3- All planning and its effects are in the hands of the Almighty Allah; do not turn to others. «فللّه المكرجمیعاً»

4- Human scheming against Allah is meaningless, because scheming and trickery only work where the target of the scheme is unaware. «یعلم ما تكسب»

Ayah

وَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَسْتَ مُرْسَلًا ۚ قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ شَهِيدًۢا بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكُمْ وَمَنْ عِندَهُۥ عِلْمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ (٤۳)
The faithless say, ‘You have not been sent [by Allah].’ Say, ‘Allah suffices as a witness between me and you, and he who possesses the knowledge of the Book.’ (43)

Key Points

In the first Ayah of this Surah, we read that what is revealed to the Prophet is the truth, even if most people do not believe. In this last Ayah, we also read that the disbelievers deny the Prophet's mission. This indicates the peak of obstinacy and enmity of the people of falsehood towards the Divine Messenger and His true word. When one of the companions of Hadrat Sulayman (Solomon), who possessed a portion of the knowledge of the Book, could bring the throne of the Queen of Sheba before him in less than the blink of an eye, then what power could someone who possesses the entire knowledge of the Book have?! According to narrations, the one who possesses the knowledge of the entire Book is referring to Hadrat Ali عليه السلام and the Household of the Prophet (Ahl al-Bayt) علیهم السلام.(196)

196) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- Faith in Allah's support is the best backing against any denial or humiliation. «قل كفى باللَّه»

2- Awareness of the Divine Book elevates a person's status to the point where his testimony is placed alongside the testimony of Allah. «كفى باللَّه... ومن عنده علم الكتاب»

3- Sometimes the value of one person's testimony is greater than the denial of thousands. (The principle lies with quality, not quantity). «یقول الّذین كفروا لست مرسلا قل كفى باللّه شهیداً... من عنده علم الكتاب»
